programme.title: Grand Designs Australia
programme.desc: Tani Richards has some fascinating pastimes. As well as practising yoga and horse riding, she has a passion for all things medieval, including dressing up like Maid Marion for a bit of horse back archery! After selling her and pilot husband Tim's medieval style family home known as the 'Sand Castle' in Perth's City Beach, she has plans to build an 'earth castle' on their 60 acre property north of Perth. But this is not your standard earth castle! Built into the sides of a man-made dam, there are plans for a drawbridge, turret and even a moate. With underground rooms and tunnels, and gothic features and finishes, the footprint is vast- nearly 2000 square metres in total. But Tani's endless modifications, coupled with fallout from the pandemic take a toll on the budget and the timelines- not to mention many of Tani's dreams for her medieval fortress!

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: The team are in Northern Ireland to meet married couple Catherine and Nigel. Following the passing of Catherine's mother, they made the bold decision to downsize; Nigel loves it, but Catherine wants to move.

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: The team are in Cambridgeshire to meet Amber and Martin. On the outside, their three-bed semi, where they live with son Milo, looks like any normal family home. But the inside tells a very different story.

programme.title: Location Location Location
programme.sub-title: East London, Brighton And Hove Revisits
programme.desc: This week, we're catching up with three house hunters, with two very different needs, but one shared goal. In 2018, schoolteacher Katy and her comedian husband Charlie, baby Lola, and pet pooch Harper were on the hunt for their first family home in Brighton. Having moved in with Katy's mum to save for a deposit, they had not only grown used to home comforts, but also the vibrant village atmosphere of pricey Fiveways; an area where the property dreams didn't match their budget. With Phil's help, they widened the net, viewing four fantastic finds in and around Brighton. Two years later, Phil's back to see whether life on the coast has been plain sailing. And in 2019, Kirstie was scouring East London with 'granny on the go' Janet. Having been diagnosed with a degenerative back condition, Janet was desperate to ditch her lift-less, third storey flat in exchange for a ground-floor forever home. The tricky East London property market was proving tough to navigate though, and Kirstie soon found out about Janet's hidden desire for a project. One year on, Phil's eager to see if she got her wish.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location Australia
programme.desc: This week we're in double trouble in one of Australia's toughest markets with two couples who can't afford to take a wrong step. We're on the hunt for houses in some of Melbourne's most popular suburbs with Helen and Tom, a couple of downsizers whose business went bust last year, and also Robbie and Bec, who are trading Melbourne's hip bars for the burbs on a tiny budget. But buying houses can be an emotional business and both couples will find adapting to change is not so easy.

programme.title: Selling Houses Australia
programme.desc: Amanda, Paul and David are siblings who sadly lost their doctor parents in the MH17 plane crash over Ukraine in July 2014. They are selling their five-bedroom family home on a bushy 12 acres near Toowoomba. A touching story as the family is well-loved and respected in the area. Also, they're prepared to donate some of the profits to Medicines Sans Frontiers.

programme.title: Selling Houses Australia
programme.desc: In a program first, bushwhackers Winty, Albone and Blaze attempt to modernise a derelict church from the 1920s. But location and building makes this no easy sale.

programme.title: Love It Or List It Australia
programme.desc: Joel and Anna live in the worst house on the best street and after plans to knock it down fell through, are now living in a space that is not conducive to their lifestyle with two small children.

programme.title: Grand Designs Australia
programme.desc: Architect Ariane dreams of a home that blurs inside and out and her methods are as unconventional as her materials. Will she convince her husband it has all been worth it, and stay within a $500,000 budget?

programme.title: Phil Spencer: New Zealand's Best Homes

programme.title: Grand Designs Australia
programme.desc: Soaring property prices have put inner-city living out of the reach of many. But not for architect Ben and artist wife Tania, who decide to think laterally and get creative buying a tiny 64 square metre discarded scrap of land in one of North Melbourne's grittier back lanes. Their plans - to build a three storey, three bedroom, two bathroom home with a large garden - all for the not-so-grand price of $600,000. But their slim budget is in total contrast to their expansive ideas. Ben is working on a revolutionary concreting method using a giant robot that can transform boring concrete walls into soaring works of art. And while it's an interesting idea in theory, using their own family home to test it is high stakes indeed! With the family crammed into rental accommodation while Ben finesses his master work, the pressure mounts while the months drag on. Will his plans to transform concrete buildings into habitable architecture works of art ever become a reality?

programme.title: Hotel Paranormal
programme.desc: A sceptic becomes a believer after a spectral bride appears on a hotel balcony, ghostly orphans attack a father and daughter in New Orleans, and a man fights off the spirit of a serial killer.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: Take a look inside the doors of a family-run pawnshop in Las Vegas, where three generations of men from the Harrison family, grandfather Richard, son Rick and grandson Corey jointly run the business, with clashing and camaraderie every step of the way. This family lives and breathes the pawning business, using their sharp-eyed skills to carefully assess the value of items their colourful customers bring in, objects ranging from the obscure to the truly historic.

programme.title: Shipping Wars
programme.desc: Follow independent shippers who have discovered that fortunes can be made by transporting items that traditional carriers won't touch. Each episode of Shipping Wars dives into the cutthroat world of these heavy-duty movers as they battle for the chance to transport the un-shippable. The competition begins with uShip, the world's largest online auction house for independent truckers. From oversized loads and bulky packages to the truly bizarre, these shippers will haul anything if the price is right. From rookie haulers to seasoned veterans, no shipment is too big for these professionals to handle. Marc, Jarrett, Jennifer, Roy and his beloved cat Muffy are back, along with new cast members Chris and Robbie. One of the industry's most unique shippers, Chris's edge in the business comes from his truck, which runs off of a waste vegetable oil system that he invented himself. While he's on the road, his girlfriend and business partner Robbie books loads and maps out routes while taking care of her son, Carter. Don't let their age fool you, this South Carolina couple juggles parenthood, running a successful business, and taking care of their golden retriever, Gix, who is a permanent passenger on the road.

programme.title: Car Crash TV
programme.desc: Geoff Bird's back with Yorkshire Parking, we play The Blame Game, and enjoy The Art of the Automobile. Watch Abra Car Dabra and see drivers handle city driving.

programme.title: Car Crash TV
programme.desc: We explore Bikers, Multi-lane Mayhem, and Stock Car Collisions, before playing the Blame Game. We examine the Perils of Pedestrians and Perilous Public Transport.

programme.title: Paranormal Survivor
programme.desc: Paranormal activity in Lisa Blakely's home puts her son in harm's way, a shadowy figure terrorises a young boy, and Micky Bailey falls victim to an evil spirit intent on inflicting harm.

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: The series reaches its majestic climax. Who will win Greg Davies's coveted golden head to become the seventh Taskmaster Champion in history?

programme.title: Junior Taskmaster
programme.desc: It is the second and last semi-final, and the youthful competitors go all out to impress Rose with their task-based tenacity in a show packed with tension, caterpillars and a bit of detective work.

programme.title: Rose Matafeo Comedy Special
programme.desc: Rose Matafeo returns for her second stand-up special, leaning into her insightful humor to share candid takes on relationships, supporting friends and the differences between herself and her parents.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: The outback is one of the world's last wild frontiers. It's vast, untamed and home to some of the toughest places to reach. For the truckers who travel this harsh country, there's only one way to get from A to B: the hard way.

programme.title: Alone

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: Take a look inside the doors of a family-run pawnshop in Las Vegas, where three generations of men from the Harrison family, grandfather Richard, son Rick and grandson Corey jointly run the business, with clashing and camaraderie every step of the way. This family lives and breathes the pawning business, using their sharp-eyed skills to carefully assess the value of items their colourful customers bring in, objects ranging from the obscure to the truly historic.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men

programme.title: Truck Night In America
programme.desc: This program makes history with the toughest truck obstacle course ever built. America and trucks go together and, since 1925 when the first American factory-produced truck rolled off the line, American owners have been devising ingenious ways to make them faster, stronger, and better. Five truck owners will compete in a series of gruelling challenges that test their craftsmanship and driving ability to the max. Each episode features unique challenges that test trucks in a way that's never been seen before. The challenges culminate in The Green Hell, a three-mile obstacle course which includes challenges that send trucks flying off jumps, over a mountain of crushed cars and even through a snake-infested swamp. Helping them along the way are four experts in the off-road world - they coach, they build, they fix, but the one thing they can't do is drive. In the end, the truck who finishes is crowned the champion - if they can make it that far. In this episode, the Big Dogs are back, along with incredible craftsmanship and massive tires, featuring a 1951 Chevy pick-up and 1971 GMC pick-up. The Silo Pulldown and Four Horsemen show no mercy in vanquishing trucks, and it's a run for the ages in the Green Hell.

programme.desc: The Mackay Brothers hit the bush and try to dodge destruction. Steve Grahame takes on an old enemy at the worst possible time of the year. Bumpa Farrell struggles for power on a hay convoy of epic proportions.

programme.title: Aussie Pickers
programme.desc: Join Lucas Callaghan and Adam McDonald, Australia's own pickers, as they scour the country's back roads and barnyards in the quest to turn one man's trash into another man's treasure. Along the way we meet some of Australia's most authentic and colourful characters, whose stories reveal a rich Australian history, and what the pickers find will surprise and astound you.

programme.title: Spectacular Spider-Man
programme.desc: An animated television show that focuses on a sixteen-year-old Peter Parker, and the origins of Spider-Man.

programme.title: Todd McFarlane's Spawn
programme.desc: With a serial killer on the loose, Wanda's search for her husband Terry hits a detour when Wynn's men follow and corner her. Appearing on the scene to eliminate the agents, Spawn allows Wanda to see him for the first time - but she's horrified.

programme.title: Todd McFarlane's Spawn
programme.desc: While Lisa Wu is investigating the murders of two other reporters, Terry is arrested by the police. Wynn blames Merrick for all his recent problems finding Terry; as punishment, he sends another agent to kill Wanda and Cyan.

programme.title: Shipping Wars
programme.desc: Follow independent shippers who have discovered that fortunes can be made by transporting items that traditional carriers won't touch. Each episode of Shipping Wars dives into the cutthroat world of these heavy-duty movers as they battle for the chance to transport the un-shippable. The competition begins with uShip, the world's largest online auction house for independent truckers. From oversized loads and bulky packages to the truly bizarre, these shippers will haul anything if the price is right. From rookie haulers to seasoned veterans, no shipment is too big for these professionals to handle. Marc, Jarrett, Jennifer, Roy and his beloved cat Muffy are back, along with new cast members Chris and Robbie. One of the industry's most unique shippers, Chris's edge in the business comes from his truck, which runs off of a waste vegetable oil system that he invented himself. While he's on the road, his girlfriend and business partner Robbie books loads and maps out routes while taking care of her son, Carter. Don't let their age fool you, this South Carolina couple juggles parenthood, running a successful business, and taking care of their golden retriever, Gix, who is a permanent passenger on the road.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: Take a look inside the doors of a family-run pawnshop in Las Vegas, where three generations of men from the Harrison family, grandfather Richard, son Rick and grandson Corey jointly run the business, with clashing and camaraderie every step of the way. This family lives and breathes the pawning business, using their sharp-eyed skills to carefully assess the value of items their colourful customers bring in, objects ranging from the obscure to the truly historic.

programme.desc: Spawn takes the fight to Wynn, telling him that he is coming for him at midnight. Seeing his schemes beginning to crumble, Wynn attempts to have his agents kill Lisa Wu as she draws closer to the truth.

programme.title: Todd McFarlane's Spawn
programme.desc: Spawn is tormented by nightmares of a Medieval Black Knight hunting him and demanding that he acknowledge which Master he serves. A mysterious prisoner is introduced; we find he is being held by Jason Wynn.

programme.title: Big Easy Motors
programme.desc: From a 1957 Chevy Bel Air convertible, to a 1967 Shelby Mustang GT, Charles Handler turns piles of junk into custom cars. He searches all over Louisiana, including the backwoods and the bayou, for classic cars no one wants, invests his own money to fix them up and restore them, and then sells them to the highest bidder. Their cars can sell for upwards of $50,000, so there's a huge profit to be made. But then again, if they put in the work and the car doesn't sell for what they expect it to, they could come home with no profit at all.

programme.title: I Kissed a Girl
programme.desc: Meg and Fiorenza deal with their big secret; Naee has multiple love interests; new girl Eva turns heads; everyone has a big decision to make at the first Kiss-Off.

programme.title: Beverly Hills: Real Housewives Of...
programme.desc: The sand settles after Oceanside, but the ladies return home with unresolved feelings. Garcelle enjoys her recently finished beach house. Erika navigates deep-rooted emotions.

programme.title: NYC: Real Housewives Of...
programme.desc: The Puerto Rico trip continues as the women shop and explore San Juan. Erin reveals an explicit picture of Abe to Jenna and Brynn that draws criticism from others.

programme.title: Million Dollar Listing NY
programme.desc: Fredrik lands the assignment of a lifetime - a building with $400 million dollars in potential sales - but his boss Howard thinks he may have over-promised in order to get it. Meanwhile, Ryan realises that when you have two sellers, you can't please one without making the other upset. Downtown, Steve gets a list of ridiculous demands from a broker and makes one of his own - a list of new buyers that he discovered via his social media.

programme.title: Million Dollar Listing NY
programme.desc: In the hopes of selling a $20 million unit in his new mega-tower, Fredrik calls on everyone he knows, including reaching out to a blast from the past: seasoned agent Michael Lorber. Ryan goes toe to toe with his buyer's wingman, social media influencer The Fat Jewish, on a number of showings. Meanwhile, Steve quickly realises that when you mix business with friendship, you may end up with neither in the end.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: A Night's Watch deserter is tracked down outside of Winterfell, prompting swift justice by Lord Eddard 'Ned' Stark and raising concerns about the dangers in the lawless lands north of the Wall. Returning home, Ned learns from his wife Catelyn that his mentor, Jon Arryn, has died in the Westeros capital of King's Landing, and that King Robert is on his way north to offer Ned Arryn's position as the King's Hand.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: When Bran miraculously survives his fall from the tower, Cersei and Jaime scramble to ensure he will not live to expose their treachery. Having agreed to become the King's Hand, Ned leaves The North with daughters Sansa and Arya, while Catelyn stays behind to tend to Bran. Jon Snow heads north to join the brotherhood of the Night's Watch. Tyrion decides to forgo the trip south with his family, instead joining Jon in the entourage heading to the Wall. Viserys bides his times in hopes of winning back the throne, while Dany focuses her attention on learning how to please her new husband, Drogo.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: At Castle Black, Jon Snow impresses Tyrion at the expense of greener recruits; suspicious that the Lannisters had a hand in Bran's fall, Catelyn covertly follows her husband to King's Landing, where she is intercepted by Petyr Baelish, aka Littlefinger, a shrewd long-time ally and brothel owner; Cersei and Jaime ponder the implications of Bran's recovery.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: Ned looks to a book for clues to the death of his predecessor, and uncovers one of King Robert's bastards. Jon takes measures to protect Samwell from further abuse at Castle Black and a frustrated Viserys clashes with Daenerys in Vaes Dothrak. Sansa imagines her future as a queen, while Arya envisions a far different future. Catelyn rallies her husband's allies to make a point, while Tyrion finds himself caught in the wrong place at the wrong time.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: Incensed over news of Daenerys' alliance with the Dothrakis, Robert orders a pre-emptive strike on the Targaryens that drives a wedge in his relationship with Ned. A captive Tyrion helps Catelyn, but receives a cold reception at the Eyrie from her sister, John Arryn's widow Lysa.

programme.title: Dune: Prophecy
programme.desc: As Tula contends with his true identity, Valya's maneuvering leads her into an epic confrontation with an increasingly powerful Desmond.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: Reinstated as the Hand, Ned sits for the king while Robert is on a hunt, and issues a decree that could have long-term consequences throughout the Seven Kingdoms; at the Eyrie, Tyrion confesses to his 'crimes', and demands that Lysa give him a trial by combat; Joffrey apologises to Sansa; Viserys receives his final payment for Daenerys from Drogo.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: Explaining that the future of the Lannisters is at stake, Tywin presses Jaime to 'be the man you were meant to be' as they prepare for battle, while Ned confronts Cersei about the secrets that killed Jon Arryn. With the fate of the missing Benjen very much on his mind, Jon Snow takes his Night's Watch vows, though not with the assignment he coveted. After Ser Jorah saves Daenerys from treachery, an enraged Drogo vows to lead the Dothraki where they've never gone before, and an injured Robert takes pains to ensure an orderly transition at King's Landing.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: In the aftermath of Ned's capture, Syrio and Arya face off against Lannister guards while Cersei manipulates Sansa to her own ends. Robb rallies his father's northern allies against Tywin Lannister and heads south to war, and Tyrion forms an uneasy alliance with the hill tribes and reunites with his father. Jon lashes out at Thorne and battles a mysterious attacker from beyond the Wall. Dany is forced to reconcile her desire to conquer Westeros with Drogo's savagery after the Dothraki raid a peaceful village.

programme.title: Game of Thrones

programme.title: Game of Thrones

programme.desc: As the Wiccan-vampire stand-off reaches a critical juncture, Sookie summons her faerie powers to prevent Marnie from bewitching Bill, Eric and Pam into a suicide march, while Jesus casts a secret spell designed to unbind Antonia and break the witch's deadly defences. Sam settles a score with Marcus, while Alcide confronts Debbie about her allegiances, and Andy finds unexpected passion in the forest. Lafayette is consumed by the past.

programme.title: John From Cincinnati
programme.desc: Cass wakes up to a hard drive full of video footage she has no idea what to do with, but Cissy, on the other hand, confronts her problems head on, harping at Mitch over the phone about Tina's conspicuously timed appearance in Imperial Beach; Joe drives John and Bill to the road where he found John stabbed and abandoned, scouring the area through binoculars in search of his assailant.

programme.title: The Paris Murders
programme.desc: A young woman is found dead with her mouth stapled shut. Did someone want to silence her?

programme.title: In Treatment
programme.desc: Brooke struggles to draw genuine answers out of her newest patient Laila, who's been dragged to therapy by her concerned grandmother over recent 'life choices'.

programme.title: In Treatment
programme.desc: After failing to honour a commitment, Brooke receives an unexpected visit from her friend Rita, worried that she is teetering on the edge of a bad decision.

programme.title: Divorce
programme.desc: After Robert finds Tom in a compromising position, Frances and Robert revisit their marriage counsellor to set new ground rules for the kids. Frances weighs how serious her own new relationship with Andrew should be. Jackie seeks out Dallas' advice during a chance encounter at Diane's country club.

programme.title: Divorce
programme.desc: Frances spots the dashing art bigwig Skip Zakarian at an auction, and takes the opportunity to invite him to Sylvia's upcoming show, where he shows interest in more than art. A successful sale inspires Jackie to propose that she and Robert team up on a house-flipping business, but he has qualms about the idea.

programme.title: Dune: Prophecy
programme.desc: While Tula tries to keep her secret project from being discovered by the other Sisters, Desmond goes on a warpath to root out insurgents.

programme.title: Dune: Prophecy
programme.desc: As Tula contends with his true identity, Valya's maneuvering leads her into an epic confrontation with an increasingly powerful Desmond.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: As Robb Stark and his northern army continue the war against the Lannisters, Tyrion arrives in King's Landing to counsel Joffrey and temper the young king's excesses. On the island of Dragonstone, Stannis Baratheon plots an invasion to claim his late brother's throne, allying himself with the fiery Melisandre, a strange priestess of a stranger god. Across the sea, Daenerys, her three young dragons, and khalasar trek through the Red Waste in search of allies, or water. In the North, Bran presides over a threadbare Winterfell while, beyond the Wall, Jon Snow and the Night's Watch must shelter with a devious wildling.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: In the wake of a bloody purge in the capital, Tyrion chastens Cersei for alienating the king's subjects; on the road north, Arya shares a secret with a Night's Watch recruit named Gendry. With supplies dwindling, one of Dany's scouts returns with news of their position; after nine years as a Stark ward, Theon Greyjoy reunites with his father Balon and sister Yara, who want to restore the ancient kingdom of the Iron Islands; Davos enlists Salladhor Saan, a pirate, to join forces with Stannis and Melisandre for a naval invasion of King's Landing.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.sub-title: What Is Dead May Never Die
programme.desc: At the Red Keep, Tyrion plots three alliances through the promise of marriage; Catelyn arrives in the Stormlands to forge an alliance of her own, but King Renly, his new wife Margaery, and her brother Loras Tyrell, have other plans in mind; Balon Greyjoy maps out his strategy of attack; at Winterfell, Luwin tries to decipher Bran's dreams.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: Joffrey punishes Sansa for Robb's victories, while Tyrion and Bronn scramble to temper the king's cruelty. Catelyn entreats Stannis and Renly to forego their ambitions and unite against the Lannisters, and Dany and her exhausted khalasar arrive at the gates of Qarth, a prosperous city with strong walls and rulers who greet her outside them. Tyrion coerces a queen's man into being his eyes and ears. Arya and Gendry are taken to Harrenhal, where their lives rest in the hands of 'The Mountain.' Gregor Clegane. Davos must revert to his old ways and smuggle Melisandre into a secret cove.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: The end of the Baratheon rivalry drives Catelyn to flee and Littlefinger to act. At King's Landing, Tyrion's source alerts him to Joffrey's flawed defence plan and a mysterious secret weapon. Theon sails to the Stony Shore to prove he's worthy to be called Ironborn. In Harrenhal, Arya receives a promise from Jaqen H'ghar, one of three prisoners she saved from the Gold Cloaks, while the Night's Watch arrive at the Fist of the First Men, an ancient fortress where they hope to stem the advance of the wildling army.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.sub-title: The Old Gods And The New
programme.desc: Theon completes his master stroke, and in King's Landing, the Lannisters send Myrcella from harm's way in the nick of time. Arya comes face to face with a surprise visitor, and Dany vows to take what is hers. Robb and Catelyn receive crucial news, while Qhorin gives Jon a chance to prove himself.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: Jaime meets a distant relative, Daenerys receives an invitation to the House of the Undying, Theon leads a search party. Jon loses his way in the wilderness, and Cersei counsels Sansa.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: At Winterfell, Theon receives a visitor and holds down the fort. Arya calls in her final debt with Jaqen in a way that displeases him. Robb is betrayed and Tyrion and Varys find common ground. Dany ignores Jorah's advice. Stannis and Davos approach their destination, and Davos is offered a reward.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: Tyrion and the Lannisters fight for their lives as Stannis' fleet assaults King's Landing.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: In the Season 2 finale Tyrion awakens to a changed situation and King Joffrey doles out rewards to his subjects. As Theon stirs his men to action, Luwin offers some final advice. Brienne silences Jaime and Arya receives a gift from Jaqen. Dany goes to a strange place, while Jon proves himself to Qhorin.

programme.title: The White Lotus
programme.desc: After promising to have a more positive attitude, Harper gets looped into a side excursion with Daphne, leaving Ethan and Cameron behind for some bro time.

programme.title: Dune: Prophecy

programme.title: True Blood
programme.desc: It's Samhain, Wicca's greatest holy day, and spirits of the dead surface in Bon Temps, giving Sookie some valuable allies to combat Marnie's newest incarnation. Lafayette's latest medium encounter imperils his relationship with Jesus, and Jason finds confession good for the soul but not the body. Alcide makes a heartfelt appeal to the woman he loves, and Terry receives an unexpected visitor at Merlotte's. Sam and Luna envision a storybook ending for once, and Nan wears out her welcome with Bill and Eric. Spirits of the dead converge on Bon Temps as Sookie and her two favourite vampires battle Marnie's latest incarnation.

programme.title: John From Cincinnati
programme.desc: Early on the sixth day since John's arrival, Butchie Yost is trying to get back in the game. With Kai looking on from a hiding spot on the beach, Butchie attacks a succession of waves, but some spectacular wipe-outs are showing his rust. Before long, though, the lapsed pro starts landing his tricks, and Kai slinks off to her friend Jane's house to pick up his old boards. From the creators behind Deadwood and surf-noir writer Kem Nunn, this Showcase Original Presentation is set in Imperial Beach, California, the last great surf-break before Tijuana. This is a story about the Yosts; a family of surfers whose awesome athletic talents have seemed for generations to come with a curse attached. The Yosts' reign and reputation has been eroded by years of bad luck, addiction and hubris. A stranger named John arrives - and the Yosts' banal existence is lifted into something profound, miraculous and, possibly, universal. The distinctive theme song is by the late Joe Strummer and the Mescaleros.

programme.desc: Butchie takes his surfing re-immersion up a notch by sharing the water with another pro, Sonny Mac. programme.title: NCIS

programme.title: Cold Case
programme.desc: When a corrupt cop commits suicide, questions are raised about the guilt of a death-row inmate. Misplaced evidence from the original trial indicates the wrong man was implicated.

programme.title: Cold Case
programme.desc: In 1945, a journalist left a note telling someone to meet her at a train station where she later died. Rush finds she was dating a man who said he was a Jew who escaped the Nazis.

programme.title: FBI
programme.desc: As the team searches for an abducted college co-ed, Jubal becomes intent on obtaining lifesaving leads from the father of the abductors previous victim.

programme.title: FBI
programme.desc: Maggie goes undercover working in a bar to investigate an anti-government group that regularly gathers there and has several members suspected in a series of bombings.

programme.title: NCIS: Hawai'i
programme.desc: When an anti-capitalist protester is murdered, Jane and the team investigate and find themselves caught in a war between ecoactivists and a tech billionaire fighting over a piece of land.

programme.title: NCIS: Hawai'i
programme.desc: The team investigate a cold case involving WWII-era bones on the 80th anniversary of the Pearl Harbor bombings, and are shocked to learn they belong to a 100-year-old survivor of the attack.

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.sub-title: White Trash, Holy Rollers And Punching People
programme.desc: After Missy punches a boy in Sunday school for making fun of her family, Pastor Jeff insists Mary take a leave of absence from working at the church to the objection of both George and Pastor Rob. At home, George disagrees with Mary that Missy should be punished. Pastor Rob stops by and prays with Mary, but Missy comes home interrupting their alone time. Mandy tells Connie her family has disowned her for being pregnant and invites her to accompany her to her medical examination. At the clinic, Georgie showed up despite being uninvited and after some bickering was allowed to stay, while Connie offers to cover the cost of Mandy's prenatal care. George is upset that Mary saw Pastor Rob again in private and storms out of the door. After Sheldon informs President Hagemeyer of his family troubles, Mary snaps at President Hagemeyer over the phone. Missy hugs and supports her mother, later covering for George's absence to her brothers and asking God to bless Mary.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Beverly looks to set an example for her feuding sons by reuniting Coach Mellor and his estranged older brother. Murray enlists Erica's boyfriend to keep her from having too much fun in college.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: As Adam's campus visit exposes the truth about Erica's college experience, Murray and Beverly are at odds when she looks to start a new career.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Charles' ex-wife tries to interfere with his relationship with Genevieve; Amy asks Terry for a recommendation letter; Holt and Rosa turn to Gina for help with a case.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Captain Holt and Jake contract the mumps during an investigation and decide to be quarantined together. Rosa tries to help Boyle mourn and Terry cleans up Hitchcock and Scully's mess.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Walden may lose Louis when the social worker finds out that he and Alan aren't actually a couple, but Alan isn't ready to give up without a fight.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Walden joins a support group for adoptive fathers, and learns it's nice to be away from Alan.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Walden worries about Louis and the adoption process when Alan breaks up with the social worker in hopes of getting back together with Lyndsey.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: Elaine has tickets for a historical clothing exhibition but realises that she has no female friends. Jerry suggests she ask Susan. Kramer says that is the wrong move because George's worlds will collide. Jerry meets his pool guy outside a movie, then can't get rid of him.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: Jerry finds the number of a girl he wants to contact on Kramer's AIDS walk sponsor list. After finding out that her preferred method of birth control is only available in a limited supply, Elaine must decide whether her current boyfriend is worthy. George tells Susan the secret of the size of Jerry's jeans and that leads to a fight about all secrets; he tells her another of Jerry's secrets when they make up. Jerry finds out that George told Susan one of his secrets and decides he's out of the loop.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: When Deacon and Kelly buy a vacation home, Carrie is upset because they could have easily afforded it, but they were too cheap.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: After almost running over a chicken on the highway, Doug becomes more animal-sensitive and decides to become a vegetarian.

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.sub-title: Uncle Sheldon And A Hormonal Firecracker
programme.desc: Mary and George are still bickering. After Connie tells Sheldon and Missy that Mary isn't pregnant, Missy figures out the truth about Georgie. Mary swears the twins to secrecy. Talking about it without specifics, Mary later feels judged by Pastor Jeff. Sheldon cannot hold it and tells an uninterested Dr. Linkletter, George tells Coach Wilkins, Mary tells Pastor Rob and Missy tells a girl at school. Georgie is upset everyone spread it around and Mandy is further angry with him. Georgie has a heartfelt moment with Missy who believes Georgie will be a good father. George is further irritated Mary bonded with Pastor Rob again over this and listens to him when George had earlier given essentially the same advice; but doesn't tell her why he dislikes Pastor Rob. When nobody attends her Bible Study, a sad Mary calls Pastor Rob who says he always has time for her.

programme.title: Parks and Recreation
programme.desc: Leslie, Ben, and Chris visit Eagleton to discuss financial matters with city councillor Ingrid de Forest. Meanwhile, Ann takes April to vet school orientation and Ron works to get off the grid.

programme.desc: Drew wins a vacation with Kate, but Lewis reveals Drew's feelings for Kate, causing tension when Oswald stows away on their flight to the Bahamas.

programme.desc: After a beer tank explodes in the Clemens' yard, Drew tries to make amends, while Janet convinces Greg to invest in Buzz Beer, causing tension in the group.

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: In an attempt to find out what Robin's new girlfriend is like, Rebecca asks Sam to oversee she and Robin at an outing, while Sam continues to try and get Rebecca into bed.

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Rebecca is taken by surprise when she finds out Robin had used her personal corporate account to gain information about the company.

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Frasier's show is subjected to a focus group evaluation, and 11 of the 12 participants have nothing but rave reviews for his work. But one man just doesn't like him, throwing Frasier into a frenzy as he desperately tries to find out why.

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: On the third anniversary of Frasier's radio show, Roz gives Frasier a cassette of their very first broadcast. At home, he falls asleep while listening to the tape, and has flashbacks of his first days back in Seattle.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Will is reluctant to introduce his ballet dancer boyfriend to Grace. Meanwhile, Jack suggests to his company that he design their Christmas window.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Will & Grace try to lure Tina, Will's dad's mistress, away from Will's father by setting her up with another man. Unfortunately, the only man available is Will's gay friend, Larry.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: When Frank starts showing a keen interest in Ray's work and is constantly in a cheery mood, everyone questions his radical change in behaviour. Ray soon uncovers the real reason behind Frank's transformation.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray and Debra are discouraged when they realise that their sex life isn't what it used to be. They approach Frank and Marie about the situation, however, soon wish they never asked.

programme.title: The Office
programme.desc: A new assistant's incompetence has the Dunder Mifflin staff up in arms, but Michael refuses to fire him, as he's his nephew. Andy tries to ignore Erin's dating of Gabe.

programme.title: The Office

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Arthur accidentally starts a fire in Doug and Carrie's basement, so Carrie then begins to think that her and Doug should find him a retirement home to go live in. When Doug starts to clean out the basement by removing Arthur's things, Doug sees that Arthur used to be an actor and he finds out that he gave up the career for Carrie. This gives Doug second thoughts on giving Arthur the boot.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: When Carrie insists that Doug figure out how to invest their tax return cheque, Doug decides to buy an ice-cream truck from a stranger.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: Jerry turns in Uncle Leo for shoplifting, while George is forced to buy an expensive book he was caught reading in the store's bathroom.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: As George sets out to preserve his high score on an old arcade game, a serial killer's presence forces Jerry into a relationship he doesn't like.

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: In an attempt to find out what Robin's new girlfriend is like, Rebecca asks Sam to oversee she and Robin at an outing, while Sam continues to try and get Rebecca into bed.

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Rebecca is taken by surprise when she finds out Robin had used her personal corporate account to gain information about the company.

programme.desc: Frasier's show is subjected to a focus group evaluation, and 11 of the 12 participants have nothing but rave reviews for his work. But one man just doesn't like him, throwing Frasier into a frenzy as he desperately tries to find out why.

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: On the third anniversary of Frasier's radio show, Roz gives Frasier a cassette of their very first broadcast. At home, he falls asleep while listening to the tape, and has flashbacks of his first days back in Seattle.

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.sub-title: A Clogged Pore, A Little Spanish And The Future
programme.desc: George finds out that the high school offered his job to Coach Wilkins and quits. Working at Dale's store won't be enough to make ends meet, so George and Mary both hit the newspaper's classifieds, without much luck. To George's surprise, Mary later gets a job working with Brenda Sparks at the bowling alley after revealing she is desperate for one good thing. Georgie wants to make extra money for the baby and comes up with the idea to sell cigarettes in the gaming room. Connie goes with him to Mexico for a cheap supply. Connie later calls George, saying that she and Georgie got arrested at the border. Meanwhile, Sheldon gets his first pimple, alarming him about puberty and pending adulthood. President Hagemeyer and Dr. Linkletter end up missing their youth. Sheldon later has a nightmare that he becomes each of his parents, Connie and Georgie, all adults who seem miserable to him. Missy is able to calm Sheldon, getting him to focus on things he's looking forward to in adulthood and that.

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.sub-title: Four Hundred Cartons Of Undeclared Cigarettes And A Niblingo
programme.desc: George Sr. bails Meemaw and Georgie out of jail. Also, Sheldon and Missy meet Mandy and Mary feels unwelcome at church, in the sixth season premiere of Young Sheldon.

programme.sub-title: Future Worf And The Margarita Of The South Pacific

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: Paul Murton continues his odyssey, discovering the secrets of some of the most remote places in Europe. His first journey takes him to the largest island in Britain: the long island of Harris and Lewis. Paul tries his hand at weaving Harris tweed, meets the family behind the world famous Stornoway black pudding and uncovers the remarkable story of how a mysterious medieval chess set was discovered on the remote beach of Uig on Lewis.

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: Thirty miles from mainland Scotland, there is a chain of low-lying islands that face the full might of the Atlantic Ocean: the Uists and Benbecula. On these islands there seems to be more water than land as a spectacular patchwork of freshwater lochs and pools pockmark the landscape. But it's the unique ecology of these unspoilt islands that makes them so special. Paul's journey sees him try his hand at fishing for wild sea trout, uncover the fascinating story of how South Uist became a missile testing ground, and discover the secrets of the beautiful but deserted tidal island of Vallay.

programme.title: Taken Together: Who Killed Lyric And Eli
programme.desc: Drew Collins confronts someone who may link a suspect to the Evansdale murder scene.

programme.title: Casey Anthony: Where the Truth Lies
programme.desc: Casey Anthony revisits memories from the period when her daughter Caylee went missing. As she slowly pieces together clues, Casey finally admits what she suspects happened that fateful day.

programme.title: The Jinx: Life & Deaths Of Robert Durst
programme.desc: Susan Berman was the daughter of a famous Las Vegas mobster, Davey 'The Jew' Berman, who was a partner with 'Bugsy' Siegel in The Flamingo Hotel. Robert Durst's closest friend since they attended UCLA together, she had a mobster's mentality and matching code of ethics; people said she would do anything for a friend. Since Kathie Durst's disappearance two decades earlier, Robert Durst had been the subject of media and public scrutiny, and she had become his spokesperson to the media. Some say that Robert and Susan kept each other's secrets, and that if anyone knew what happened to Kathie, it would be Susan.

programme.title: Video Killed the Radio Star
programme.desc: Robert Elms discusses the amazing body of music video work from Bjork. Featuring interviews with directors Chris Cunningham, Michel Gondry and Spike Jonze, and videos All is Full of Love, It's Oh So Quiet and Bachelorette.

programme.title: Too Young To Die
programme.desc: He only lived to be 28 years old. Heath Ledger was a star that didn't really want to be one, opting for roles in small films and declining many major offers.

programme.title: Michael Palin: Travels of a Lifetime
programme.desc: Michael revisits his fourth travel series, Sahara with Michael Palin (2002), a four-episode trek round the Sahara desert.

programme.title: World's Most Scenic Railway Journeys
programme.desc: The Cambrian Line starts in Pwllheli and travels 200km, taking in the fairytale village of Portmeirion, the seaside resort of Aberdovey and the ancient capital of Machynlleth.

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: Paul Murton continues his odyssey, discovering the secrets of some of the most remote places in Europe. His first journey takes him to the largest island in Britain: the long island of Harris and Lewis. Paul tries his hand at weaving Harris tweed, meets the family behind the world famous Stornoway black pudding and uncovers the remarkable story of how a mysterious medieval chess set was discovered on the remote beach of Uig on Lewis.

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: Thirty miles from mainland Scotland, there is a chain of low-lying islands that face the full might of the Atlantic Ocean: the Uists and Benbecula. On these islands there seems to be more water than land as a spectacular patchwork of freshwater lochs and pools pockmark the landscape. But it's the unique ecology of these unspoilt islands that makes them so special. Paul's journey sees him try his hand at fishing for wild sea trout, uncover the fascinating story of how South Uist became a missile testing ground, and discover the secrets of the beautiful but deserted tidal island of Vallay.

programme.title: Branson
programme.desc: The series premiere explores Branson's early years and the influence of his iconoclastic mother. As a dyslexic teenager, he leaves school at age 15 to create a magazine called Student, leading to a mail-order record business and eventually Virgin Records and Virgin megastores.

programme.title: Taken Together: Who Killed Lyric And Eli
programme.desc: Drew Collins confronts someone who may link a suspect to the Evansdale murder scene.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Portillo meets the craftsmen behind the Victorian furniture trade, discovers how George Bradshaw helped save Britain's canal heritage, and sees Shakespeare as a 19th-century railway tourist.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Portillo travels to the manufacturing centres of the Midlands. He learns how the railways helped to make Birmingham the pen-making capital of the world, hears the chilling tale of one of 19th-century Britain's most notorious murderers and samples the delicacies concocted in a Victorian kitchen at Shugborough Hall.

programme.title: Icons Unearthed: Star Wars
programme.desc: Marcia Lucas, Oscar-winning film editor and ex-wife of George Lucas, sits down for her first-ever on camera interview and provides unique insight into the rise of Lucasfilm.

programme.title: Artist to Icon
programme.desc: Features interviews with Whitney Houston's The Bodyguard director Mick Jackson, musician Wayne Linsey, The Preacher's Wife producer Elliot Abbott, plus many more.

programme.title: Music's Greatest Mysteries
programme.desc: The musical fallout from the night disco died, a rock God breathes life into Tina Turner's career and we explore the otherworldly occurrences in Rick Rubin's recording studio.

programme.title: Music's Greatest Mysteries
programme.desc: Willie Nelson gets over on the IRS, the overlooked band that may have invented punk music and we look into the Frank Sinatra song that has spawned a dozen murders.

programme.title: Landscape Artist of the Year
programme.desc: In this 'best-of' episode we get to observe the most unusual artistic techniques, experience the challenges of painting 'en plein air', and indulge in stunning British landscapes.

programme.title: Portrait Artist of the Year
programme.sub-title: Ken Stott, Mike Leigh, Lydia Leonard
programme.desc: In this seventh heat, nine artists are challenged to create a portrait of one of three celebrities: director Mike Leigh and actors Ken Stott and Lydia Leonard.

programme.title: The Man Who Stole The Scream
programme.desc: This gripping story details the 1994 theft of Edvard Munch's The Scream during the Winter Olympics, featuring the chase by authorities and Scotland Yard, focusing on the convicted thief Pal Enger.

programme.title: Master of Light
programme.desc: George Anthony Morton is a classical painter who spent ten years in federal prison for dealing drugs. While incarcerated, he nurtured his craft and unique artistic ability. Since his release, he is doing everything he can to defy society's unlevel playing field and tackle the white-dominant art world. Now, he is on a journey back to his hometown of Kansas City, where he tries to heal the broken relationship with his mother and paint family members in the style of the Dutch Old Masters. Going back home forces George to face his past in his quest to rewrite the script of his life and discover the contribution of African influences to the realist tradition.

programme.title: Christian Hook: Painting the Invisible
programme.desc: Inspired by his fascination with science, artist Christian Hook embarks on a challenge to paint the relationship between two people.

programme.title: Windsor Castle Fire 30 Years On
programme.desc: This dramatic documentary reveals the amazing personal stories behind the disastrous Windsor Castle fire that ignited 30 years ago.

programme.title: Secrets of the Queen's Coronation
programme.desc: A behind-the-scenes look at Queen Elizabeth II's coronation. This documentary shares memories and anecdotes from people who plays key roles at the celebration, all speaking publicly for the first time.

programme.title: Ren Faire
programme.desc: Jeff gains the upper hand over Louie. Darla's promotion to co-general manager leads to disastrous consequences for both Jeff and herself. George finally lands on the only successor he can trust.

programme.title: Santa Camp
programme.desc: Every summer, a horde of professional Santas, Mrs Clauses and elves descend on a campsite in the New Hampshire woods to learn the tricks of their trade. But this year is different. The organizers, members of the one-hundred strong New England Santa Society, have decided to tackle a complicated and historic problem: the lack of diversity in the Santa industry. They decide to enlist a Black Santa named Chris, a Santa with a disability named Fin, and a transgender Santa named Levi, each with their own surprising Santa origin story. Meanwhile, the Mrs Claus delegation, led by veteran Dianne, is becoming more outspoken in its calls for equal billing and pay. The arrival of the new Santas sparks a reckoning about what it means to embody the beloved role model and cultural icon. After graduation, the Clauses draw on their camp experiences to affect change in their communities. But they soon learn that not everybody is ready for Santas who "break the mold".

programme.title: Portrait Artist of the Year
programme.desc: Having made it through the semi-final, just three artists remain in the running for Portrait Artist of the Year 2020. Artists have four hours to create their portraits, and the hallowed halls of the National Portrait Gallery provide a fitting backdrop for their final sitter, renowned poet, Lemn Sissay. The artists also unveil their commission paintings of micro-sculptor Willard Wigan; landscape architect Kim Wilkie and chef Nieves Barragan Mohacho.

programme.title: The Brief History Of Cricket Downunder
programme.desc: By the early 90s, players excel in one-day cricket, becoming specialists who transform the game and its dynamics.

programme.title: The Australian Dream: Adam Goodes
programme.desc: The Australian Dream is a documentary that uses the remarkable and inspirational story of Indigenous AFL legend Adam Goodes as the prism through which to tell a deeper and more powerful story about race, identity and belonging. The film will unpick the events of the 2013-15 AFL seasons and ask fundamental questions about the nature of racism and discrimination in society today. Walkley award-winning writer Stan Grant and BAFTA award-winning director Daniel Gordon join forces to tell this remarkable story of one of the most decorated and celebrated players in AFL history. A man who remains a cultural hero; the very epitome of resilience and survival, who continues to fight for equality and reconciliation.

programme.title: Back On The Record With Bob Costas
programme.desc: Interviews with the biggest names in sports, entertainment and popular culture, and discussions led by Bob Costas that will address today's most topical sports issues.

programme.desc: Who really wrote Rap's first hit? The Legends of the House of the Rising Sun? How did Cher battle the US Navy?

programme.sub-title: Lost Beatle, Stonehenge, And Bad Press
programme.desc: John, Paul, George and Jimmie. Was Stonehenge built for music? Ashanti's marketing fiasco.

programme.title: Landscape Artist of the Year
programme.desc: In this third heat, they're off to Castle Ward in Northern Ireland, where eight artists are faced with the gothic facade of this historic mansion.

programme.title: Portrait Artist of the Year
programme.desc: Having made it through the semi-final, just three artists remain in the running for Portrait Artist of the Year 2020. Artists have four hours to create their portraits, and the hallowed halls of the National Portrait Gallery provide a fitting backdrop for their final sitter, renowned poet, Lemn Sissay. The artists also unveil their commission paintings of micro-sculptor Willard Wigan; landscape architect Kim Wilkie and chef Nieves Barragan Mohacho.

programme.title: The Brief History Of Cricket Downunder
programme.desc: By the early 90s, players excel in one-day cricket, becoming specialists who transform the game and its dynamics.

programme.title: The Australian Dream: Adam Goodes
programme.desc: The Australian Dream is a documentary that uses the remarkable and inspirational story of Indigenous AFL legend Adam Goodes as the prism through which to tell a deeper and more powerful story about race, identity and belonging. The film will unpick the events of the 2013-15 AFL seasons and ask fundamental questions about the nature of racism and discrimination in society today. Walkley award-winning writer Stan Grant and BAFTA award-winning director Daniel Gordon join forces to tell this remarkable story of one of the most decorated and celebrated players in AFL history. A man who remains a cultural hero; the very epitome of resilience and survival, who continues to fight for equality and reconciliation.

programme.title: Back On The Record With Bob Costas
programme.desc: Interviews with the biggest names in sports, entertainment and popular culture, and discussions led by Bob Costas that will address today's most topical sports issues.

programme.title: Landscape Artist of the Year
programme.desc: In this third heat, they're off to Castle Ward in Northern Ireland, where eight artists are faced with the gothic facade of this historic mansion.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo gets close to some precious Victorian botany at Kew Gardens, tries his hand at croquet, and discovers a very surprising 19th-century place of worship.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael learns how volunteer Victorian firefighters liked a tipple, discovers how 19th-century sewage pumps were a celebration of design, and puts in a shift at the oldest fish market in Britain.

programme.title: Icons Unearthed: Star Wars
programme.desc: Marcia Lucas, Oscar-winning film editor and ex-wife of George Lucas, sits down for her first-ever on camera interview and provides unique insight into the rise of Lucasfilm.

programme.title: Stars of the Silver Screen
programme.desc: Glenn Close is an American actress with a career spanning over four decades. She established herself as a Hollywood leading lady with roles in Fatal Attraction (1987) and Dangerous Liaisons (1988), both of which earned her nominations for the Academy Award for Best Actress.

programme.title: The Brief History Of Cricket Downunder
programme.desc: By the early 90s, players excel in one-day cricket, becoming specialists who transform the game and its dynamics.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Portillo discovers how derelict Victorian London is being rejuvenated, puts in a shift at a Cambridgeshire brick factory, and meets the immigrant community built from its bricks.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo sees how Lincolnshire farmers utilised rails to improve their harvests, visits Lincoln's most impressive cathedral, and looks to the future of rail freight.

programme.title: Icons Unearthed: Star Wars
programme.desc: Marcia Lucas, Oscar-winning film editor and ex-wife of George Lucas, sits down for her first-ever on camera interview and provides unique insight into the rise of Lucasfilm.

programme.title: Stars of the Silver Screen
programme.desc: Timothy Francis Robbins was born October 16, 1958 and is an American actor, filmmaker, and musician. He is known for portraying Andy Dufresne in the film The Shawshank Redemption, and has won an Academy Award and two Golden Globe Awards for his roles in the films The Player (1992) and Mystic River (2003).

programme.title: Music's Greatest Mysteries
programme.desc: Did the CIA dose Deadheads? What is a K-Pop's Black Ocean? What is 'You're So Vain' really about?

programme.title: Music's Greatest Mysteries
programme.desc: The secret kidnapping of Jimi Hendrix. Slipknot vs Burger King. Did a failed music career create David Koresh?

programme.title: Landscape Artist of the Year
programme.desc: The contestants are hoping to create work that catches the eye of judges at the famous roller-coasters of Blackpool Pleasure Beach.

programme.title: Portrait Artist of the Year
programme.desc: From over 1000 applicants, 72 artists made it to the heats, 8 made it to the semi-final and just 3 to the final, where our worthy winner for this sixth series of Portrait Artist of the Year was selected. In this final programme, the winner embarks on a 10,000 pounds commission to create a portrait of music legend, Nile Rodgers. Charged with painting this formidable character, behind so many iconic tracks that have filled dancefloors across the world since the 1970s, can our artist capture the man behind the music? We see our winning artist build a relationship with the sitter, witness the development of the painting, and hold our breath as the final portrait is unveiled at London's Royal Albert Hall. Stephen Mangan and Joan Bakewell are our presenters.

programme.title: Elon Musk's Twitter Takeover
programme.desc: The story of Elon Musk's long and often troubled relationship with Twitter, charting his journey from one of the site's most provocative users to its sole proprietor.

programme.title: Project Greenlight: A New Generation
programme.desc: A docuseries focusing on the next generation of talented female filmmakers who are given the chance to direct a feature film. Issa Rae will appear in every episode as an Executive Producer, providing guidance and mentorship to these aspiring filmmakers.

programme.title: Faye
programme.desc: Hollywood legend Faye Dunaway candidly discusses the triumphs and challenges of her illustrious career. Through those reflections, she explores personal discoveries, including her struggles with mental health issues.

programme.title: Project Greenlight: A New Generation
programme.desc: A docuseries focusing on the next generation of talented female filmmakers who are given the chance to direct a feature film. Issa Rae will appear in every episode as an Executive Producer, providing guidance and mentorship to these aspiring filmmakers.

programme.title: Europe's Waterways
programme.sub-title: Germany - From Lake Muritz Via Berlin To The Floodplains Of The Spree
programme.desc: Venturing upstream on Europe's waterways, the series discovers five regions of the continent from an unknown perspective and visits people who live from, on and with water. Rivers, canals, lake regions: they have shaped Europe's cultures for centuries - they are a place to live, transport arteries and sources of energy. Five journeys on watercourses broad and narrow lead to Italy, Germany, France, Poland, Holland and Belgium. Along the way: new projects for the future of water in Europe.

programme.title: Video Killed the Radio Star
programme.desc: An eye-opening behind-the-scenes look at the making of Depeche Mode's most notable music videos.

programme.title: Video Killed the Radio Star
programme.desc: A fascinating look at the secrets and ideas behind the most iconic music videos ever made.

programme.title: Too Young To Die
programme.desc: "If he ever lived to be 30, I'd be surprised", his mother once said in an interview. Sadly, she was right. On April 5, 1994, the 27-year-old rock star killed himself in his house in Seattle.

programme.title: The Canary Islands
programme.sub-title: La Gomera And El Hierro - A Land Before Time
programme.desc: Off the African coast in the middle of the Atlantic, underwater volcanoes raged for millions of years and created a volcanic archipelago: the Canary Islands. Each of them has its own face. This series tells of natural spectacles, unique natural areas and the formation of the volcanic archipelago.

programme.title: World's Most Scenic Railway Journeys
programme.desc: The Dovre line runs from Oslo to the remote northern wonderland of Trondheim, passing through the vast Gudbrandsdal valley and across the awe-inspiring Dovrefjell mountain plateau.

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: On the west coast of Scotland are a group of islands that are so remote they almost seem to have been forgotten by the rest of the world and are populated by just a few hardy souls. Crossing the Sea of the Hebrides Paul sets off to explore the Shiants; heads east to the romantic Isle of Ewe, and on to the Summer Isles, before travelling to the mighty cliffs of Handa. This tour sees Paul go diving at Scotland's first 'scallop ranch', kayak to the Summer Isles, and visit the eerie but beautiful Island of Gruinard which was once considered to be so solitary and remote, it was chosen to be the testing ground for Britain's biological warfare experiments during World War II.

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: Among the islands of the Hebrides there's one that can rightly claim to have a separate identity; Islay which was once the capital of the ancient Lordship of the Isles. This programme sees Paul travel from Finlaggan where the famous ruler Somerled once held court, along Islay's windswept coast, through its historic ports and villages to end on its highest mountain. Along the way he will join a dedicated bunch of Ileach who are keeping the seafaring traditions of the island alive, discover the secrets of Islay's illicit whisky distilling past and travel to the stunning Mull of Oa on the island's south Coast, before climbing Ben Vicar for a breath-taking view across the Hebrides.

programme.title: Mystify Michael Hutchence
programme.desc: "Mystify Michael Hutchence" is a powerfully intimate and insightful portrait of the internationally renowned frontman of INXS, Michael Hutchence. Deftly woven from an extraordinary archive of rich imagery, Michael's private home movies and those of his lovers, friends, and family, the film delves beneath the public persona of the charismatic Rock God and transports us through the looking glass to reveal a multifaceted, intensely sensitive and complex man. A violent event strikes Michael and changes his life dramatically, fracturing his sense of self and robbing him of his connection to life. Amidst the darkness, Michaels newborn daughter, Tiger, becomes his one bright light. "Mystify Michael Hutchence" is written and directed by Richard Lowenstein, director of the majority of both INXS and Michael's music videos and the 1986 feature film, "Dogs In Space", in which Michael played the lead.

programme.title: Kylie: Infinite Disco
programme.desc: A one-off promotional live stream concert by Kylie Minogue in support of her 15th studio album, DISCO.

programme.title: Lizzo: Live in Concert
programme.desc: Celebrate the new year with Emmy and three-time GRAMMY-winning superstar Lizzo, her band The Lizzbians and The Little Bigs, The Big Grrrls with special guests Cardi B, SZA and Missy Elliott for a spectacular show filled with lots of love, positivity and incredible music.

programme.title: Branson
programme.desc: The episode tracks the launch of Virgin Atlantic and Branson's efforts to attract media attention to his brand, including an attempt to break the transatlantic speed record in a boat, and his balloon adventure across the Atlantic in 1986.

programme.title: Video Killed the Radio Star
programme.desc: Broadcasters Mark Ellen and Robert Elms discuss the impact of the first four Roxy Music albums. The popular British band were influential through the 70s and 80s.

programme.title: Video Killed the Radio Star
programme.desc: An in-depth look back at some of Peter Gabriel's most iconic music videos, including his 1986 hit Sledgehammer, which won a record nine MTV Video Music Awards and was a hit amongst his fans.

programme.title: Too Young To Die
programme.desc: Many followed the meteoric rise of this young man who was mercurial with an extreme personality. It only took seven films to make John Belushi unforgettable.

programme.title: Michael Palin: Travels of a Lifetime
programme.desc: Michael revisits his journey through the Himalaya, travelling through Pakistan, India and Nepal, meeting the Dalai Lama along the way.

programme.title: World's Most Scenic Railway Journeys
programme.desc: From Tirano in Italy, this route spirals 1km up the Alps, past glaciers and through mountains, ending at the base of the majestic Matterhorn.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: The Germans plan to show Hogan a weapon that will win them the war and then send him to the US to demand surrender.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: Hogan must destroy an anti-aircraft defence system designed by a beautiful Englishwoman who defected to Germany.

programme.title: Tea for Two
programme.desc: A wealthy heiress, Nanette Carter bets her uncle $25,000 that she can say no to everything for 48 hours. If she wins, she can invest the money in a Broadway show.

programme.title: High Society
programme.desc: A blue blood tries to win back his ice goddess ex-wife.

programme.title: Maverick
programme.desc: Beau discovers a woman's relatives plan to institutionalise her.

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: A priest and government agents using a 'safe' house get Jessica into a complicated case.

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.sub-title: A Very Good Year For Murder
programme.desc: Murder intrudes on a wealthy vintner's 75th birthday party, and Jessica begins one of the most personally difficult investigations of her career.

programme.title: The Good Life
programme.desc: After Tom brainstorms an idea for revamping the cultivator into a transport vehicle, the Goods try their hand at becoming a housekeeping service to the Leadbetters to earn cash.

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Fletch fancies himself as a bit of an agony aunt and is called upon by his fellow inmates to help out with affairs of the heart.

programme.title: The Goodies
programme.desc: While away on a fun-filled vacation, the Goodies unexpectedly find themselves competing in the Tour de France.

programme.title: To the Manor Born
programme.desc: A rare tropical bird nesting on the estate causes a flurry of interest among local naturalists. Audrey in unimpressed - it's a bee-eater, and she has just gone into honey production.

programme.title: Oliver!
programme.desc: Much, much more than a musical. The adaptation of Charles Dickens' famous novel, recounting the life of an orphan who runs away from the orphanage.

programme.title: Abbott and Costello Meet Frankenstein
programme.desc: The Wolfman tries to help foil Dracula's attempts to transfer Lou's brain into the ailing Frankenstein monster.

programme.title: Tequila Sunrise
programme.desc: This action movie stars Mel Gibson and Kurt Russell as lifelong friends who are on the opposite sides of the law. One is a drug dealer Dale "Mac" McKussic (Gibson) trying to go straight and his close friend, Nick Frescia (Russell) is a detective assigned to investigate his buddy. Both fall in love with a beautiful restaurateur, Jo Ann (Michelle Pfeiffer) who finds herself caught in the middle of all the drama and danger.

programme.title: Maverick
programme.desc: Beau discovers a woman's relatives plan to institutionalise her.

programme.title: Get Smart
programme.desc: Weary of waiting for Max, 99 agrees to marry the casino boss, Victor Royal.

programme.title: Get Smart
programme.desc: Max accidentally gives a Kaos code to a Kaos agent at a carnival, and is given a plutonium kewpie doll.

programme.title: The Goodies
programme.desc: While away on a fun-filled vacation, the Goodies unexpectedly find themselves competing in the Tour de France.

programme.title: Mork & Mindy
programme.desc: Mork bucks hard for a promotion from leader Orson on planet Ork, and unintentionally comes up with a report on how to stay happily married on Earth.

programme.title: Wanted: Dead or Alive
programme.desc: Josh arrives in town thinking the sheriff summoned him to go after a man.

programme.title: Wanted: Dead or Alive
programme.desc: Josh rides to Barney Durant's horse business to rent a couple of horses right after Barney pushes his son David to leave.

programme.title: Jeopardy!
programme.desc: Grant DeYoung, Gregg Meller and Ivvone Zhou try to guess the right questions.

programme.title: Jeopardy!
programme.desc: The Emmy-winning quiz show features a unique answer-and-question format. Adam Hersh vs. Kevin Laskowski vs. Carolyn Gramling.

programme.title: Maverick
programme.desc: Bret's freedom hinges on a card game against Bart.

programme.title: Get Smart
programme.desc: Max and 99 must locate a secret lab maintained by the mastermind, Dr Yes.

programme.title: Gomer Pyle
programme.desc: Sergeant Hacker siphons gas out of Sergeant Carters car, so Carter thinks that the car is getting poor mileage and sells it to Hacker for a song.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Fonzie is the best man at the wedding of an old pal, but the bride-to-be stops the ceremony after spotting Fonzie. Meanwhile, KC awaits word about a part in the school musical.

programme.title: McHale's Navy
programme.desc: Captain Binghamton assigns his aide, Lt Carpenter, as executive officer and spy in the camp of his enemy McHale. The move leaves Captain Binghamton with a predicament what to do with Ensign Parker, whom Carpenter has replaced. His first mistake is to make Parker the base maintenance officer. Written by Allan Manings. Directed by Hollingsworth Morse.

programme.title: Rawhide
programme.desc: Facing a drought, Favor is being pressured by a corrupt and revengeful acquaintance Jess Hode for first half and then all of his herd for access to water. However, when Hode's son comes to realize the truth about his dad, he tries to help.

programme.title: Steptoe and Son
programme.desc: Albert has been coughing a lot and Harold accompanies him to the hospital where they both submit to having X-rays taken. The pair get into a tizzy following a mix-up with the results.

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Mr Rumbold is called to a management seminar and makes Mr Grainger acting departmental manager. Mr Grainger soon makes himself very unpopular with the staff.

programme.title: The Day of the Jackal
programme.desc: It is the early 1960s in France. The remaining survivors of the aborted French Foreign Legion have made repeated attempts to kill President DeGaulle. The result is that he is the most closely guarded man in the world. As a desperate act, they hire The Jackal, the codename for a hired killer who agrees to kill the French president for half a million dollars. We watch his preparations, which are so thorough we wonder how he could possibly fail, even as we watch the French police attempt to pick up his trail.

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: While observing the guests at a dinner party held by a Beverly Hills gossip magazine editor, Jessica envisions the plot for her next murder mystery.

programme.desc: Bret's freedom hinges on a card game against Bart.

programme.title: The Day of the Jackal
programme.desc: It is the early 1960s in France. The remaining survivors of the aborted French Foreign Legion have made repeated attempts to kill President DeGaulle. The result is that he is the most closely guarded man in the world. As a desperate act, they hire The Jackal, the codename for a hired killer who agrees to kill the French president for half a million dollars. We watch his preparations, which are so thorough we wonder how he could possibly fail, even as we watch the French police attempt to pick up his trail.

programme.desc: Richard is anxious to sign a contract with the owner of a cosmetic company. To ward off her amorous advances, he pretends to be married to Audrey, who takes the opportunity to make some changes to the estate.

programme.title: Blazing Saddles
programme.desc: The railroad's got to run through the town of Rock Ridge. How do you drive out the townsfolk in order to steal their land? Send in the toughest gang you've got and name a new sheriff who'll last about 24 hours. But that's not really the plot of Blazing Saddles, just the pretext. Once this lunatic film gets started, logic is lost in a blizzard of gags, jokes, quips, puns, howlers, growlers and outrageous assaults upon good taste or any taste at all.

programme.title: Escape from Alcatraz
programme.desc: Alcatraz is the most secure prison of its time. It is believed that no one can ever escape from it, until three daring men make a possibly successful attempt at escaping from one of the most infamous prisons in the world.

programme.title: The Man Who Would Be King
programme.desc: Two British soldiers in India decide to resign from the Army and set themselves up as deities in Kafiristan - a land where no white man has set foot since Alexander.

programme.title: Maverick
programme.desc: Bart has a lot of explaining to do after a bank robbery.

programme.title: Get Smart
programme.desc: The Chief wants to impress the writer of a magazine in hopes that an article on Control will result in a better national image and a bigger budget.

programme.title: Get Smart
programme.desc: Albert J Pfister has developed a deadly silent explosive.

programme.desc: Potsie sets Richie up with a girl known to have a reputation. Richie doesn't get far with her but leads Fonzie and others to believe he did.

programme.title: Happy Days

Covered in 60 kilos of fondant, the clothing and facial features must match the show's leading man. Suzanne must impress with a detailed cake replica of the Lloyd's of London's Lutine Bell- which forms the focal point for a Remembrance Day service. programme.title: Jamie At Home Christmas
programme.desc: In this special, Jamie takes the strain out of Christmas dinner and allows you to reclaim your Christmas day. By following Jamie's advice you can be calm, organised and prepared and your Christmas dinner will be a sure-fire winner. Good ingredients are paramount and Jamie has a visit from his geese and turkey suppliers who advise us what to look for in a good bird. Jamie and Brian, his gardener, look at the greens on offer in the garden and Gennaro, Jamie's Italian mentor, drops in to roast some chestnuts. Jamie walks us through the elements of the perfect roast dinner and tips on how to prepare and cook your birds, how to roast the perfect potatoes, dress the perfect greens and make sensational mince pies. By following Jamie's advice and tips your Christmas culinary preparations should be stress-free forever more.
