programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: The various parts for the vacuum are made at different work stations all over this huge site, but Gregg is starting with the base.

programme.title: The Great Australian Bake Off
programme.desc: It's finals week and the pressure is on for the three remaining bakers. The Bake Off grounds are turned into a celebratory garden party as the bakers throw everything they've got into the final bake.

programme.title: Gino's Italy: Secrets of the South
programme.desc: Gino heads back to Puglia's capital Bari and the town of Cisternino.

programme.title: Jamie's Family Christmas
programme.desc: Jamie concludes his guide to Christmas with his tips for stress-free parties, soups and cheese fondue, as well as some delicious pomegranate shots and incredible mulled cider.

programme.title: Food Unwrapped Best Of Specials
programme.desc: Some favourite superfood stories sorting fact from fiction, from prunes, microgreens and horchata, to caffeine and weight loss. Plus, could too much kale be a bad thing?

programme.title: Masterchef UK: Celebrity
programme.desc: Across six weeks, John Torode and Gregg Wallace will be putting their own taste buds through a sensory assault course, as they discover which celebrities have the palate and skills.

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Ultimate Home Cooking
programme.desc: Gordon turns his focus to cooking brilliant picnic food for a great day out. Gordon's youngest daughter, Tilly, helps make a delicious prawn and cucumber salad, and a chocolate and marshmallow fridge cake.

programme.title: Jamie's 15 Minute Meals
programme.desc: The recipes are crispy duck, hoi sin lettuce parcels, plus chicken cacciatore, and spaghetti and smoky tomato sauce.

programme.title: Mary Berry Cooks
programme.desc: Mary is back in her kitchen to cook her favourite meal, Sunday lunch. The star of the show is slow roast shoulder of lamb with rosemary and paprika rub. For pudding, Mary serves plum tarte tatin and a delicious fruit salad.

programme.title: Jamie's Sugar Rush
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver investigates sugar's huge contribution to global health problems like obesity and type-2 diabetes, reveals how much sugar is in healthy-looking food, and explores what can be done to help.

programme.title: Eat Well For Less
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace and award winning greengrocer Chris Bavin and are on a mission to help families save money, sort food facts from food fiction and eat well for less.

programme.title: Paul Hollywood Eats Japan
programme.desc: Paul's spending his first week in Japan's mega city capital, Tokyo. He has a lesson in Japanese restaurant etiquette, sees Godzilla and visits one of the worlds cheapest Michelin starred restaurants. He sniffs out an extraordinary bakery which puts a very big smile on his face, then samples the Japanese practice of solo dining and the even weirder practice of solo Karaoke. Finally, Paul heads off on a karting tour of the city with a man called Lady Beard. After fishing for their lunch, they cuddle some puppies, risk life and limb on the streets of Tokyo and finish Paul's first week with a three-course dinner up the posh end of a jumbo jet well, sort of.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Alison Hammond and Noel Fielding host as the bakers take on the challenges of caramel week. In the signature challenge the contestants craft some biscuits before taking on a caramel technical.

programme.desc: Gordon turns his focus to cooking brilliant picnic food for a great day out. Gordon's youngest daughter, Tilly, helps make a delicious prawn and cucumber salad, and a chocolate and marshmallow fridge cake.

programme.title: Mary Berry Cooks
programme.desc: Mary is back in her kitchen to cook her favourite meal, Sunday lunch. The star of the show is slow roast shoulder of lamb with rosemary and paprika rub. For pudding, Mary serves plum tarte tatin and a delicious fruit salad.

programme.title: Eat Well For Less NZ
programme.desc: This week's family shop with a plan and know how to balance their books but with busy jobs and at least one very fussy eater, mealtimes are a constant struggle. Will Corrinne let chef Michael Van de Elzen and restauranteur Ganesh Raj overhaul her pristine kitchen and will her hubby be able to live-up to her cuisine standards?

programme.title: Extreme Cake Makers
programme.desc: Today, cake artist Michelle's cake world almost comes crashing down as she tackles a 3ft wide rotating planet earth cake to celebrate the 40th anniversary of a travel guide. With the cake needing to appear at two parties, this globe includes 2 identical vanilla cake tops so it can be cut twice, and the planet's surface is covered in 20 kilos of accurately detailed royal icing. Phil and Christine must miniaturise their skills, as they tackle their smallest wedding cake to date for a couple who've eloped to Cornwall to get married. Consisting of 2 tiny Mr and Mrs cakes made in their favourite flavours of chocolate fudge and lemon drizzle, they're surrounded by a 'moon gate' of sugar flowers and each one is decorated to match their wedding outfits. Molly makes a spectacular life-like, regal leopard cake, complete with a sugar paste crown, to help raise money at a charity auction.

Molly makes a cake take of a favourite farmyard animal when she's asked for a life-like donkey as a surprise 50th birthday cake.

programme.title: Jeremy Pang's Asian Kitchen
programme.desc: In the kitchen today, Jeremy is making dishes to feed and please a crowd, starting with delicious Steamed bao with Black Pepper Beef Shortrib filling - a perfect party platter!

programme.title: French Gourmet with Justine Schofield
programme.desc: Justine dives into Dijon's vibrant market, shares her beloved apple crepes, and explores mustard-making at La Moutarderie Fallot.

programme.title: French Gourmet with Justine Schofield
programme.desc: Impossible to traverse Burgundy without indulging in wine, Justine concocts a wine-infused gourmandise. A visit to La Table Maison Lobbies unfolds the secrets of two local delights.

programme.title: Rachel Khoo's My Swedish Kitchen
programme.desc: Rachel heads into the woods in search of mushrooms. Rachel meets an ice-cream maker who inspires her to experiment with unusual flavours as she makes a lemon and dill parfait.

programme.title: Farm to Fork
programme.desc: Courtney and Michael create a must-try recipe at home, their flavourful traybake kung pao cauliflower. Meanwhile, Louis showcases his culinary skills by preparing a mouthwatering crispy skin chicken.

programme.title: Farm to Fork
programme.desc: Courtney dazzles with her culinary skills, presenting BBQ baby peppers with feta yoghurt and coriander salad, as well as a delectable silverbeet and feta pie.

programme.title: Paddock To Plate
programme.desc: Matt Moran travels to South Australia's Eyre Peninsula, a region of both untamed coastline and harsh, arid desert. Local sheep graze on the area's saltbush, becoming a meat flavoured from the inside out. Building on the salt theme, Matt bakes the lamb in a salt crust pastry. South in Port Lincoln, it's time to learn how to catch squid, then on to Coffin Bay for oysters. Thanks to Matt, blue fin tuna is on the menu for lucky racegoers at The Kimba Cup race day.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Alison Hammond and Noel Fielding host as the bakers take on the challenges of caramel week. In the signature challenge the contestants craft some biscuits before taking on a caramel technical.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine With Me
programme.desc: Helen and her outspoken Albanian boyfriend, David, kick off the competition, hoping their Greek menu will bag them the grand.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine With Me
programme.desc: In Essex, three rival duos battle it out to host the best dinner party and win a 1000-pounds prize.

programme.title: Jamie's One Pan Wonders
programme.desc: Jamie cooks two ideal one-pan meals: shakshuka with asparagus and broccoli, and delicious pulled pork that can be batched and frozen for future meals.

programme.title: Tom Kerridge's Sunday Lunch
programme.desc: Tom transforms his favourite Chinese dishes into a delicious Sunday feast. First up, he makes aromatic crispy duck served with pancakes and homemade plum sauce.

programme.title: River Cottage Summer Collection
programme.desc: Intrepid chef Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall brings a back-to-nature attitude to modern cooking and consumption. As his mission encompasses real people with real food issues, the host takes viewers into homes around Britain to show ordinary folks how easy and enjoyable it can be to move from pre-processed foods to nutritious natural products.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: Rick Stein heads north towards the Arctic Circle to explore the culinary delights of Reykjavik. A perfect winter break enriched with a smorgasbord of the best Iceland has to offer.

programme.title: James Martin's Home Comforts
programme.desc: James' travels have provided him with lots of culinary inspiration. He makes a boozy pudding and a fantastic recipe he netted on a recent fishing trip. James is joined by special guest Antonio Carluccio.

programme.title: James Martin's Islands To Highlands
programme.desc: James is joined by chef Lisa Goodwin-Allen and they pay a visit to the Isle of Man during the famous TT race warm up week. James serves up proper biker food- pork burgers with a spicy apple sauce, while Lisa visits some local organic juice producers.

programme.title: James Martin's American Adventure
programme.desc: James Martin starts his American adventure with a visit to one of his favourite places in the USA: the Napa Valley. First, he drops into one of the best food and drink markets in California, before cooking with some delicious local produce at a stunning vineyard. James gets an exclusive look at the kitchen at one of the world's best restaurants, The French Laundry, and tours their stunning kitchen garden with chef and owner Thomas Keller. A visit to the Napa Valley wouldn't be complete without blending your own wine, so James makes his own red to bring back home with him. James takes inspiration from Napa's bountiful larder to cook California-inspired food back in the UK.

programme.title: Hairy Bikers Go North
programme.desc: The bikers are exploring Lancashire, the red rose county. From Pendle in the east, to Blackpool in the west, Lancashire has a regional cuisine to match anywhere in the country.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Alison Hammond and Noel Fielding host as the bakers take on the challenges of caramel week. In the signature challenge the contestants craft some biscuits before taking on a caramel technical.

programme.title: How Do They Really Do It
programme.desc: This episode goes inside Subway to reveal how a US sandwich shop became the most ubiquitous fast food brand on the high street, with more outlets across the UK than McDonalds. Discovering the methods Subway uses to create its iconic sandwiches, from speciality bread to full-flavoured sauces, and the business model which has led it to dominate the market, experts and insiders uncover the secrets of its popularity.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: PiperNet finds Monica suspicious of an early success, and the team must race against the clock as their future is threatened. Realising he's made more enemies than friends, Richard makes a surprising move.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Richard discovers his promise to keep Pied Piper free from collecting user data is under threat, while Jared finds himself missing his role as Richard's go-to guy.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Richard meets a potential investor, while Gilfoyle butts heads with HR. Gavin explores a leaner Hooli's future.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: The Pied Piper team races to close a major deal, while Dinesh considers being a better person. An angry Jared reluctantly helps Richard.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: When Richard's authority is threatened by his former Hooli manager, Dinesh has just the solution. Jared learns some hard truths about his birth parents.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Richard fumes at Gavin's new ethical stance, Dinesh's trip turns into a nightmare, while Gilfoyle and Monica work on their peer review scores.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Richard, Dinesh, Gilfoyle and Jared face their greatest challenge yet as a massive event tests PiperNet's capabilities, while Laurie's unexpected presence makes them wary of sabotage.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Richard and the Pied Piper guys finally see the fruits of their labour, but realise that a bigger company means much bigger problems.

programme.title: Band of Brothers
programme.desc: It is two years earlier: in Camp Toccoa, GA, volunteers train for America's new military experiment, the paratroops. Theirs is one of nine companies in the 506th Parachute Infantry Regiment: E, aka Easy Company, commanded by the domineering Lt. Herbert Sobel. Through a regimen that involves frequent six-mile climbs up and down Currahee, a local mountain, Sobel succeeds in drilling the men into an elite unit. But his heavy-handed tactics, punishments for petty or imagined infractions, and strategic failures during manoeuvres, alienate Sobel from his men, who prefer his cool-headed second-in-command, Lt. Winters. After the 506th is shipped to England to complete training, Sobel harasses Winters by citing him for failing to inspect a latrine on time. When Winters requests a trial by court-martial, six of his sergeants: Salty Harris, Chuck Grant, Bill Guarnere, Carwood Lipton, Mike Ranney and Floyd Talbert request transfer rather than follow Sobel into combat.

programme.title: Band of Brothers
programme.desc: Landing in a field, Winters finds he has lost his rifle and supply bag. He teams up with a private from another company, John Hall, and sets out to find their units. During the night they link up with a few more soldiers, and ambush a horse-drawn German supply convoy, their first enemy encounter. Regrouping in a nearby town, Winters runs into Easy's Lt. 'Buck' Compton, who tells him that Lt. Meehan is unaccounted for, which puts Winters in charge. His orders are to lead an attack on a nearby cluster of German artillery pieces endangering the seaborne infantry at Utah Beach. Winters deploys a small group on the entrenched enemy positions, and eventually takes four artillery pieces in succession, disabling them with TNT. The euphoria is tempered, however, when Winters finds Hall dead, killed by machine-gun fire. The mission is successful, but Winters has lost his first man as acting company commander.

programme.title: Band of Brothers
programme.desc: Lt. Harry Welsh delivers Easy's next orders: take Carentan, the only town where armoured vehicles from Omaha and Utah beaches can link up. Winters briefs the men, and Easy heads into Carentan, encountering fierce resistance from Germans holed up in buildings. The town is eventually secured, although Winters gets hit above his ankle by ricochet gunfire.

programme.title: Band of Brothers
programme.desc: The respite doesn't last long; in September the men learn they're being airlifted to Holland as part of Operation Market-Garden, a joint American-British offensive that aims at securing quick access to Germany and ending the war by Christmas. Unlike Normandy, the daytime parachute drop is executed smoothly, and the company is greeted as heroes as they enter the town of Eindhoven after minimal resistance. All that changes when the company marches to Nuenen, where Easy is met by a brutal attack that ends with Compton wounded, a retreat ordered, and 'Bull' Randleman stranded in the town overnight. It's a sobering and bloody initiation to combat for the replacements.

programme.title: Band of Brothers
programme.desc: As Winters labours over a report on the dike mission, Heyliger leads a rescue of British soldiers escaping from the besieged town of Arnhem. An anxious Winters worries about Easy (now one of three companies he helps command), especially after Heyliger is shot and wounded by a nervous sentry. After moving off the line to France, Nixon insists that Winters take a break and see Paris. Winters is haunted there by the memory of a young German he killed at close range on the dike. As he returns to the Company at Mourmelon, news comes of a massive German counterattack in the Ardennes Forest. Winters helps Easy race there to hold the line, his men ill-equipped for the cold and the battle ahead.

programme.title: Band of Brothers
programme.desc: Medic Eugene Roe is overwhelmed, on edge and close to combat exhaustion, when he finds friendship with a Belgian nurse. Easy Company spends a miserable Christmas in the trenches, but is buoyed after receiving the news that the German army's demand for surrender was met with General McAuliffe's defiant answer: "Nuts!"

programme.title: Band of Brothers
programme.desc: Several are killed and wounded in fierce shelling, compounded by the incompetence of their new commander, Lieutenant Dike, about whom Winters can do nothing. Easy takes Foy, but at an enormous cost.

programme.title: Band of Brothers
programme.desc: Lt. Hank Jones, fresh from West Point and eager for combat experience, volunteers to lead, though he must convince a sceptical Winters. Also assigned to the patrol is Private David Webster, back in Easy after rehabilitation of an injury. While successful, the mission costs a soldier's life, prompting Winters to ignore the order to send out a second patrol the next night.

programme.title: Band of Brothers
programme.desc: A patrol in a nearby forest discovers an abandoned Nazi concentration camp, still filled with emaciated prisoners. The local citizenry, unbelievably disavowing knowledge of its existence, is made to clean it up. Suddenly, news arrives from Berlin: Hitler is dead.

programme.title: Band of Brothers
programme.desc: As German officers hand over their weapons, soldiers raid well-stocked wine cellars, snag souvenirs from residences, and endure one final tragedy when a replacement soldier cracks. Despite the fact that VE (Victory in Europe) Day has passed, Easy faces the prospect of imminent deployment to the Pacific Theatre. But as the men compare "points" to see who has earned enough to go home, word arrives that the Japanese have surrendered, ending the war and dissolving Easy Company. A memorable closing vignette tells what happened to Winters and some of his men after they returned to the States.

programme.title: Band of Brothers
programme.desc: As German officers hand over their weapons, soldiers raid well-stocked wine cellars, snag souvenirs from residences, and endure one final tragedy when a replacement soldier cracks. programme.title: The Pacific
programme.desc: In the immediate aftermath of the Japanese attack on Pearl Harbor, Marine Sgt John Basilone prepares to ship out and confront the enemy somewhere in the Pacific, while budding journalist Robert Leckie enlists in the Marine Corps. Eugene Sledge, unable to enlist because of a heart murmur, says farewell to his best friend, Sidney Phillips, who is about to leave for boot camp. Exactly eight months after Pearl Harbor, the 1st Marine Division, including Leckie and Phillips, lands on Guadalcanal in order to secure its strategically vital airfield and prepare for the inevitable counterattack.

programme.title: The Pacific
programme.desc: Basilone and the 7th Marines arrive on Guadalcanal to reinforce Leckie and the rest of the 1st Marine Division as they continue to defend the crucial airstrip. Basilone plays a key role in repelling a night time Japanese attack, but suffers a frightful personal loss. After four months of continuous action, the exhausted and disease-ridden members of the 1st Marine Division are evacuated off the island.

programme.title: The Pacific
programme.desc: Physically and mentally debilitated after the four-month ordeal on Guadalcanal, Leckie, Basilone and thousands of their comrades land in Melbourne, where they are greeted by adoring crowds and viewed as the saviours of Australia. While his buddies carouse, Leckie becomes deeply attached to an Australian woman and her first-generation Greek family. Meanwhile, Basilone is awarded the Medal of Honour and is asked to return home to help sell US war bonds.

programme.title: The Pacific
programme.desc: Finally enlisted as a marine, Sledge trains for combat at Camp Elliott. The 1st Marine Division lands at Cape Gloucester on the Japanese-held island of New Britain. As Leckie and the other marines battle the Japanese, they quickly realise that the more ominous enemy is the smothering jungle itself. Having survived Gloucester and being stationed on the godforsaken island of Pavuvu, Leckie begins displaying the physical and mental effects of combat and is sent to a naval hospital on nearby Banika for psychiatric observation.

programme.title: The Pacific
programme.desc: Basilone's celebrity grows as he travels across the country on the war bonds tour. On Pavuvu, Sledge, assigned to the 5th Marines, 1st Marine Division, is briefly reunited with Phillips and Leckie rejoins his company. Sledge then gets his first taste of combat as he, Leckie, and the rest of the 1st Marine Division meet fierce Japanese resistance while landing on the intricately and heavily defended coral island of Peleliu.

programme.title: The Pacific
programme.desc: Despite the suffocating 115-degree heat and a lack of clean drinking water, Sledge, Leckie and the other Marines confront the highly-fortified enemy as they attempt to capture the Peleliu airfield. After seeing his comrades badly injured, Leckie himself is wounded and evacuated from the island. Sledge witnesses the shocking truth about what is sometimes required to survive and fight another day.

programme.title: The Pacific
programme.desc: The Marines, including the newly christened 'Sledgehammer', continue the battle of Peleliu against an enemy determined to fight to the last man. Devastated by the loss of a revered leader, and witnessing unimaginable barbarity on both sides, Sledge veers to the very edge of moral collapse. Their objective finally secured, the Marines return to Pavuvu fundamentally changed by their experience on Peleliu.

programme.title: The Pacific
programme.desc: Increasingly frustrated by his role campaigning for war bonds, Basilone convinces the marines to allow him to train troops headed for combat. Transferred to Camp Pendleton, he enjoys a whirlwind romance with an initially reluctant female marine, Lena Riggi. But the couple know they are living on borrowed time, as Basilone is soon to take part in the marine-landing on Iwo Jima.

programme.title: The Pacific
programme.desc: After battling across the island of Okinawa for over a month, Sledge and the rest of the 1st Marine Division are ordered to relieve an Army division that has been in combat against the most strongly defended Japanese position on the island. The primordial conditions and the moral dilemma posed by the presence of civilians puts tremendous strain on the physical and psychological endurance of Sledge and the other Marines.

programme.title: The Pacific
programme.desc: After the Japanese surrender, Leckie, healed from his wounds, leaves the hospital and returns home, while Sledge heads back to Alabama to be reunited with his family and Sid Phillips. Lena visits Basilone's home and has an emotional meeting with his family. Leckie adjusts to post-war life by resuming his old job and starting a new relationship, but for Sledge, unsure why he survived the war seemingly unscathed, adjustment will require more time.

programme.title: Mr Inbetween
programme.desc: We meet our anti-hero Ray, as well as his daughter, Brittany, ex-wife, Jacinta, unwell brother, Bruce, best friend, Gary, and boss, Freddy. Freddy owes Ray money which he doesn't have, as usual so he sends Ray off with newly hired heavy, Nick, to collect and keep a 10k debt. Nick hasn't collected before and proves ineffective, so Ray has to step in and do it his way to get results. Ray meets Ally at the dog park and there is immediate chemistry - but he doesn't act on it, feeling that she's out of his league. Gary is in trouble with his Russian wife, Tatiana, over the discovery of a porn DVD and asks Ray to take the blame. While out with Brittany having ice-cream they bump into some young men who knock her ice-cream to the ground and disrespect Ray. Ray assaults one of the young men responsible and while escaping the scene he runs into Ally and takes the opportunity to make his feelings a little clearer. Ally gives Ray her number.

programme.title: Mr Inbetween
programme.desc: Ray kidnaps a man named Luke Henson and "takes care" of him out in a forest. Brittany questions her dad about Santa Claus and unicorns. The police have identified Ray as the young man's assailant; he informs Bruce that he has to go to court. Ray has his first date with Ally. It goes well. Freddy informs Ray that he was mistaken about Luke Henson. Ray is not happy about having killed the guy. Showing some moral compass and compassion Ray decides to pay Luke's widow an anonymous visit and give her some cash. Gary is in an induced coma after being bashed in a gun deal gone wrong set up by his brother-in-law, Vasilli. Vasilli's story doesn't add up and when confronted with a surprise visit by Ray, some of the truth comes out. Once Ray discovers who Gary's assailants were, he lets them know that they shouldn't mess with the people he cares about.

programme.title: Mr Inbetween
programme.desc: Gary wakes from his coma, and thanks Ray for doing "a bit of this and a bit of that" to Vassili and the guys who bashed him. Ray and Nick are becoming friends and he asks Nick for a favour. Jacinta berates Ray for telling Brittany that Jesus isn't real. The court has ordered Ray to attend anger management. His first meeting doesn't go very well. Freddy informs Ray that the guys he bashed for putting Gary in a coma belong to a crew run by his son-in-law's brother, Davros. Freddy insists Ray sits down with Davros to smooth the waters. Nick makes good on the favour. While on drugs, Vasilli breaks into Gary's house to rob the contents of his safe. Gary, who has just returned home from hospital, manages to send an SOS text to Ray who is out shopping with Ally. Ray comes to Gary's rescue and promptly deals with Vasilli - but they'll have to work out what to do with the body as Tatiana has arrived home unexpectedly.

programme.title: Mr Inbetween
programme.desc: Gary takes Ray for a drive to give him a surprise as a thank you for saving him. Ray and Ally's relationship has developed. Freddy takes Ray to meet Davros. Ray doesn't like Davros and although Davros seems placated by the end of the conference, Freddy knows better. A road-rage incident forces Ally to confront Ray about his quick access to violence, rather than attempting to diffuse a situation. When court-ordered anger management therapist Peter confronts Ray about the same impulse, Ray explains that it's justifiable to let people clearly know that there are ramifications for being an a******. For Ray, it's all about respect. Ray brings Ally home to meet Brittany. Ray can't sleep and gets up to notice a van parked outside surveilling his house. He goes for a drive and leads the van out to a safe place to do some permanent damage to it's occupants - Davros' guys - using the surprise Gary gave him.

programme.title: Mr Inbetween
programme.desc: Ray and Gary are planning to relieve a guy of his drugs and cash, and bring Nick in on the job, but a sudden turn of events scuppers their plans. After informing Freddy about his and Gary's plans going astray, Ray is picked up by Davros' slow-witted bodyguard, Bobby, and the wily hitman, Dave, who abduct Ray in his own car. Ray tells his captors about a hidden strongbox containing a great deal more cash than they're being paid to do the job. Dave devises a plan: tell Davros they ran out of gas, tell Ray that they'll let him go once the strongbox is retrieved, retrieve it, deliver Ray to Davros, and get paid twice. What could possibly go wrong? Dave and Bobby uncover the strongbox, but when they momentarily leave Ray's car unattended with Ray safely locked in the trunk, the car disappears. Dave and Bobby hot wire another car and go looking for Ray, the car, and the strongbox.

programme.title: Mr Inbetween
programme.sub-title: Your Mum's Got A Strong Box
programme.desc: We meet potheads, Scon and Lefty, and discover they are the reason for the disappearance of Ray and his car. Scon and Lefty drop off Ray's car to auto mechanic, Bernie, to be chopped for parts. Bernie is surprised to find Ray in the boot and has to dump Ray's car in a nearby forest with him still tied-up inside. Ray kicks his way out of the trunk, and shortly afterwards encounters Scon and Lefty with his phone and his strongbox. Trailing them back to Bernie's auto shop, Ray confronts the trio just as they're about to torch open his strongbox, which Ray reclaims intact. Dave and Bobby intercept Ray, and finally deliver him to Davros. As Ray prepares to be dispatched to his final resting place, Davros decides he wants in on the contents of the strongbox. Only when it's finally opened do Davros, Dave, and Bobby discover that it doesn't exactly contain what they were expecting, and are reminded that greed isn't necessarily good.

programme.title: Mr Inbetween
programme.desc: Ray's work and personal life begin to collide, and he finds himself struggling to keep both sides of his life from imploding.

programme.title: Mr Inbetween
programme.desc: Ray solves problems, but under no circumstances does he answer questions.

programme.title: Mr Inbetween
programme.desc: Ray and Ally get caught out, and good dental hygiene is as important as paying your debts.

programme.title: Mr Inbetween
programme.desc: Is there really any such thing as monsters?

programme.title: Mr Inbetween
programme.desc: All things must end, even Christmas.

programme.title: Mr Inbetween
programme.desc: Lady troubles, old friends, and hearts on trees.

programme.title: Mr Inbetween
programme.desc: Ray does favours, but doesn't forgive.

programme.title: Mr Inbetween
programme.desc: An old friend takes the fall, and an associate pays for past misdeeds.

programme.title: Mr Inbetween
programme.desc: When exiting a department store, make sure you don't leave with less than you came with.

programme.title: Mr Inbetween
programme.desc: Enemies may come and go, and so, alas, do friends.

programme.title: Mr Inbetween
programme.sub-title: There Rust, And Let Me Die

programme.title: Mr Inbetween
programme.desc: We meet our anti-hero Ray, as well as his daughter, Brittany, ex-wife, Jacinta, unwell brother, Bruce, best friend, Gary, and boss, Freddy. Freddy owes Ray money which he doesn't have, as usual so he sends Ray off with newly hired heavy, Nick, to collect and keep a 10k debt. Nick hasn't collected before and proves ineffective, so Ray has to step in and do it his way to get results. Ray meets Ally at the dog park and there is immediate chemistry - but he doesn't act on it, feeling that she's out of his league. Gary is in trouble with his Russian wife, Tatiana, over the discovery of a porn DVD and asks Ray to take the blame. While out with Brittany having ice-cream they bump into some young men who knock her ice-cream to the ground and disrespect Ray. Ray assaults one of the young men responsible and while escaping the scene he runs into Ally and takes the opportunity to make his feelings a little clearer. Ally gives Ray her number.

programme.title: Mr Inbetween
programme.desc: Ray kidnaps a man named Luke Henson and "takes care" of him out in a forest. Brittany questions her dad about Santa Claus and unicorns. The police have identified Ray as the young man's assailant; he informs Bruce that he has to go to court. Ray has his first date with Ally. It goes well. Freddy informs Ray that he was mistaken about Luke Henson. Ray is not happy about having killed the guy. Showing some moral compass and compassion Ray decides to pay Luke's widow an anonymous visit and give her some cash. Gary is in an induced coma after being bashed in a gun deal gone wrong set up by his brother-in-law, Vasilli. Vasilli's story doesn't add up and when confronted with a surprise visit by Ray, some of the truth comes out. Once Ray discovers who Gary's assailants were, he lets them know that they shouldn't mess with the people he cares about.

programme.title: Mr Inbetween
programme.desc: Gary wakes from his coma, and thanks Ray for doing "a bit of this and a bit of that" to Vassili and the guys who bashed him. Ray and Nick are becoming friends and he asks Nick for a favour. Jacinta berates Ray for telling Brittany that Jesus isn't real. The court has ordered Ray to attend anger management. His first meeting doesn't go very well. Freddy informs Ray that the guys he bashed for putting Gary in a coma belong to a crew run by his son-in-law's brother, Davros. Freddy insists Ray sits down with Davros to smooth the waters. Nick makes good on the favour. While on drugs, Vasilli breaks into Gary's house to rob the contents of his safe. Gary, who has just returned home from hospital, manages to send an SOS text to Ray who is out shopping with Ally. Ray comes to Gary's rescue and promptly deals with Vasilli - but they'll have to work out what to do with the body as Tatiana has arrived home unexpectedly.

programme.title: Mr Inbetween
programme.desc: Gary takes Ray for a drive to give him a surprise as a thank you for saving him. Ray and Ally's relationship has developed. Freddy takes Ray to meet Davros. Ray doesn't like Davros and although Davros seems placated by the end of the conference, Freddy knows better. A road-rage incident forces Ally to confront Ray about his quick access to violence, rather than attempting to diffuse a situation. When court-ordered anger management therapist Peter confronts Ray about the same impulse, Ray explains that it's justifiable to let people clearly know that there are ramifications for being an a******. For Ray, it's all about respect. Ray brings Ally home to meet Brittany. Ray can't sleep and gets up to notice a van parked outside surveilling his house. He goes for a drive and leads the van out to a safe place to do some permanent damage to it's occupants - Davros' guys - using the surprise Gary gave him.

programme.title: Mr Inbetween
programme.desc: Ray and Gary are planning to relieve a guy of his drugs and cash, and bring Nick in on the job, but a sudden turn of events scuppers their plans. After informing Freddy about his and Gary's plans going astray, Ray is picked up by Davros' slow-witted bodyguard, Bobby, and the wily hitman, Dave, who abduct Ray in his own car. Ray tells his captors about a hidden strongbox containing a great deal more cash than they're being paid to do the job. Dave devises a plan: tell Davros they ran out of gas, tell Ray that they'll let him go once the strongbox is retrieved, retrieve it, deliver Ray to Davros, and get paid twice. What could possibly go wrong? Dave and Bobby uncover the strongbox, but when they momentarily leave Ray's car unattended with Ray safely locked in the trunk, the car disappears. Dave and Bobby hot wire another car and go looking for Ray, the car, and the strongbox.

programme.title: Mr Inbetween
programme.sub-title: Your Mum's Got A Strong Box
programme.desc: We meet potheads, Scon and Lefty, and discover they are the reason for the disappearance of Ray and his car. Scon and Lefty drop off Ray's car to auto mechanic, Bernie, to be chopped for parts. Bernie is surprised to find Ray in the boot and has to dump Ray's car in a nearby forest with him still tied-up inside. Ray kicks his way out of the trunk, and shortly afterwards encounters Scon and Lefty with his phone and his strongbox. Trailing them back to Bernie's auto shop, Ray confronts the trio just as they're about to torch open his strongbox, which Ray reclaims intact. Dave and Bobby intercept Ray, and finally deliver him to Davros. As Ray prepares to be dispatched to his final resting place, Davros decides he wants in on the contents of the strongbox. Only when it's finally opened do Davros, Dave, and Bobby discover that it doesn't exactly contain what they were expecting, and are reminded that greed isn't necessarily good.

programme.title: Mr Inbetween
programme.desc: Ray's work and personal life begin to collide, and he finds himself struggling to keep both sides of his life from imploding.

programme.title: Mr Inbetween
programme.desc: Ray solves problems, but under no circumstances does he answer questions.

programme.title: Mr Inbetween
programme.desc: Ray and Ally get caught out, and good dental hygiene is as important as paying your debts.

programme.desc: Is there really any such thing as monsters?

programme.title: The Sopranos

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: Tony's friend and mob boss Jackie Aprile, is diagnosed with cancer and Christopher considers the drawbacks of providing Meadow with "safe" drugs.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: Tony investigates Dr Melfi's background and tries to appease a volatile Uncle Junior; Anthony learns about the family business when a schoolmate backs down from a fight.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: Visiting a college in Maine, Tony bonds with his daughter and spots a former mob witness; Carmela discovers Dr Melfi's gender and makes a confession to Father Phil.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: Uncle Junior takes his new role as boss seriously, irritating everyone. Tony's fascination with Dr Melfi continues as he begins having erotic dreams about her. Carmela drowns her sorrows in a shopping spree.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: As Tony and Carmela struggle with AJ, who is diagnosed with attention deficit disorder, they wonder what the children know about the family business, and Tony remembers his own childhood revelations.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: Federal indictments loom, worrying Tony and the family. Christopher's erratic behaviour disturbs Tony who confronts his nephew.

programme.desc: Tony and Uncle Junior learn secrets about each other, with results for both of Tony's families.

programme.desc: Tony recalls his first anxiety attack as a child. Christopher finally becomes a made man but struggles with the pressure of his new position.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: Svetlana enlists Russian thugs in her conflict with Janice. Dr Melfi is brutally raped, but her attacker is freed on a technicality, leaving her faced with a crisis of conscience.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: Bobby's cancer-ridden father comes out of retirement to do a hit, and Junior is given some unpleasant news about his medical condition.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: A young Bada Bing dancer named Tracee is pregnant by Ralph Cifaretto. When the two argue outside the club, Ralph brutally beats her to death. When Tony sees her lifeless body, he attacks Ralph.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: While having surgery, Uncle Junior dreams that the FBI offered him a cure for his illness if he will testify against Tony. Dr Melfi refers Carmela to a therapist who advises her to leave Tony.

programme.desc: Tony's relationship with Ralph Cifaretto remains strained. However, when Gigi dies suddenly, Tony appoints Ralph as the new captain of Gigi's crew, thereby restoring peace to the Family.

programme.sub-title: To Save Us All From Satan's Power

programme.desc: Paulie and Christopher endure a nightmarish hunt in the woods when a routine collection turns into a failed hit. Meadow harbours suspicions about Jackie Jr and catches him with another woman.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.sub-title: For All Debts Public And Private
programme.desc: With the slowing economy affecting business, New Jersey mob boss Tony Soprano invests in a potentially lucrative land deal, while Carmela frets about the family's long-term financial security.

programme.title: Sweethearts
programme.desc: Two college freshmen pull a "Turkey Dump" and break up with their high school sweethearts over "Drunksgiving" - the one chaotic night before Thanksgiving in their hometown that puts their co-dependent friendship to the test.

programme.title: How to Make Gravy
programme.desc: A feature film adaptation of Australian music legend Paul Kelly's classic song, 'How to Make Gravy'.

programme.title: Whale Nation
programme.desc: A humpback whale is beached on a remote shore. During the fight to save its life, we will discover the story of these extraordinary creatures, denizens of the world's oceans. Inspired by the best-selling book, and exquisitely narrated by Richard E. Grant, Whale Nation takes us on a journey of discovery into the unexplored territories and barely known society of whales, with their remarkable abilities and incredibly rich and complex social lives. A film about life in all its diversity, about human's place on Earth and the vital need for cooperation between the species that share our planet.

programme.title: David Holmes: The Boy Who Lived
programme.desc: A coming-of-age story of stuntman David Holmes, a prodigious teenage gymnast from Essex, England, who is selected to play Daniel Radcliffe's stunt double in the first Harry Potter film, when Daniel is just eleven. Over the next ten years, the two form an inextricable bond, but on the penultimate film a tragic accident on set leaves David paralyzed with a debilitating spinal injury, turning his world upside down. As Daniel and his closest stunt colleagues rally to support David and his family in their moment of need, it is David's extraordinary spirit of resilience that becomes their greatest source of strength and inspiration.

programme.title: Bookworm
programme.desc: 11-year-old Mildred's world is turned upside down when her estranged father, the washed-up magician Strawn Wise, comes to look after her and agrees to take her camping to find a mythological beast known as the Canterbury Panther.

programme.title: Sweet Dreams
programme.desc: Follows Morris, who must coach a misfit softball team of his fellow housemates during his mandatory stay at Sweet Dreams sober living in order to get his life back on track.

programme.title: Hypnotic
programme.desc: Determined to find his missing daughter, Austin detective, Danny Rourke instead finds himself spiraling down a rabbit hole while investigating a series of reality-bending bank robberies where he will ultimately call into question his most basic assumptions about everything and everyone in his world. Aided by Diana Cruz, an unnervingly gifted psychic, Rourke simultaneously pursues and is pursued by a lethal specter, the one man he believes holds the key to finding the missing girl only to discover more than he ever bargained for.

programme.title: Kill
programme.desc: India. When army commando Amrit finds out his true love Tulika is engaged against her will, he boards a New Delhi-bound train in a daring quest to derail the arranged marriage. But when a gang of knife-wielding thieves led by the ruthless Fani begin to terrorize innocent passengers on his train, Amrit takes them on himself in a death-defying kill-spree to save those around him - turning what should have been a typical commute into an adrenaline-fueled thrill ride.

programme.title: Dracula: Voyage of the Demeter
programme.desc: Strange events befall the merchant ship Demeter, carrying private cargo - fifty unmarked wooden crates - from Carpathia to London. Stalked each night by a merciless presence onboard the ship, when the Demeter finally arrives off the shores of England, it is a charred, derelict wreck. There is no trace of the crew.

programme.title: The Exorcist: Believer
programme.desc: When a 12-year-old girl becomes possessed by a mysterious demonic entity, her father desperately searches for help.

programme.title: The Lesson
programme.desc: Liam, an aspiring writer, accepts a tutoring position at the family estate of his idol, renowned author JM Sinclair, and his wife Helene.

programme.title: The Last Rifleman
programme.desc: World War II veteran Artie Crawford lives in Northern Ireland. After his wife's death, he decides to set off alone to France to attend the 75th anniversary of the D-Day landings.

programme.title: Golda
programme.desc: Set during the tense 19 days of the Yom Kippur War in 1973. Israeli Prime Minister Golda Meir is faced with the potential of Israel's complete destruction, and must navigate overwhelming odds, a skeptical cabinet, and a complex relationship with US Secretary of State Henry Kissinger. Her tough leadership and compassion would ultimately decide the fate of her nation and leave her with a controversial legacy around the world.

programme.title: One Life
programme.desc: The incredible true story of British humanitarian Nicholas Winton, who helps save hundreds of Central European children from the Nazis on the eve of World War II.

programme.title: Freud's Last Session
programme.desc: Freud invites iconic author C.S. Lewis to debate the existence of God, his unique relationship with his daughter, and Lewis' unconventional relationship with his best friend's mother.

programme.title: Reality
programme.desc: This film is based on the true story of a government leak from former American intelligence specialist Reality Winner. Saturday afternoon, June 2017, twenty-five-year old Reality Winner is confronted at her home by the FBI. A cryptic conversation begins and soon Reality's life starts to unravel.

programme.title: Meg 2: The Trench
programme.desc: An exploratory dive into the depths of the ocean of a research team spirals into chaos when a malevolent mining operation threatens their mission and forces them into a high-stakes battle for survival.

programme.title: Longlegs
programme.desc: In pursuit of a serial killer, an FBI agent uncovers a series of occult clues that she must solve to end his terrifying killing spree.

programme.title: Five Nights At Freddy's
programme.desc: After accepting a security guard job from Steve, Mike discovers an abandoned restaurant may be haunted by murderous animatronics.

programme.title: Silent Night
programme.desc: A father witnesses his son's death in a gang's crossfire on Christmas Eve. While recovering from a wound that costs him his voice, he makes vengeance his life's mission.

programme.title: You'll Never Find Me
programme.desc: An isolated man living at the back of a desolate caravan park is visited by a desperate young woman seeking shelter from a violent storm. As the savage storm worsens, these solitary souls begin to feel threatened, but who should really be afraid?

programme.title: Lights Out
programme.desc: A homeless veteran gets caught up in the world of underground fight clubs.

programme.title: Turtles All the Way Down
programme.desc: When a 16-year-old struggling with obsessive-compulsive disorder reconnects with her childhood crush, she confronts the possibility of finding love and happiness in the face of mental illness.

programme.title: Monkey Man
programme.desc: Inspired by the legend of Hanuman, an icon embodying strength and courage, Monkey Man stars Patel as Kid, an anonymous young man who ekes out a meager living in an underground fight club where, night after night, wearing a gorilla mask, he is beaten bloody by more popular fighters for cash. After years of suppressed rage, Kid discovers a way to infiltrate the enclave of the city's sinister elite. As his childhood trauma boils over, his mysteriously scarred hands unleash an explosive campaign of retribution to settle the score with the men who took everything from him.

programme.title: Bob Marley: One Love
programme.desc: Celebrate the life and music of an icon who inspired generations through his message of love, peace, and unity. In this powerful and uplifting film, discover Bob Marley's incredible story of overcoming adversity and the journey behind his revolutionary music that changed the world.

programme.title: The Color Purple (2023)
programme.desc: Torn apart from her sister Nettie and her children, Celie faces many hardships in her life, including an abusive husband simply called Mister. With the support of sultry singer Shug Avery and stand-her-ground stepdaughter Sofia, Celie ultimately finds extraordinary strength in the unbreakable bonds of a new kind of sisterhood.

programme.title: Oprah and the Color Purple Journey
programme.desc: In this exclusive behind-the-scenes documentary look at the anticipated new musical adaptation of The Color Purple, Academy Award-nominee Oprah Winfrey goes on location to the Georgia set to give audiences unprecedented access to the making of the new film.

programme.title: Dear Santa
programme.desc: A young boy who, in writing his yearly note to Santa, mixes up the letters and sends it to Satan instead.

programme.title: Expend4bles
programme.desc: Armed with every weapon they can get their hands on and the skills to use them, The Expendables are the world's last line of defense and the team that gets called when all other options are off the table. But new team members with new styles and tactics are going to give "new blood" a new meaning.

programme.title: Turtles All the Way Down
programme.desc: When a 16-year-old struggling with obsessive-compulsive disorder reconnects with her childhood crush, she confronts the possibility of finding love and happiness in the face of mental illness.

programme.title: Sweet Dreams
programme.desc: Follows Morris, who must coach a misfit softball team of his fellow housemates during his mandatory stay at Sweet Dreams sober living in order to get his life back on track.

programme.title: Wonka
programme.desc: Based on the extraordinary character at the center of Charlie and the Chocolate Factory, Wonka tells the wondrous story of how the world's greatest inventor, magician and chocolate-maker became the beloved Willy Wonka we know today. A young Willy Wonka, chock-full of ideas and determined to change the world one delectable bite at a time - proving that the best things in life begin with a dream, and if you're lucky enough to meet Willy Wonka, anything is possible.

programme.title: Dune: Part Two
programme.desc: Dune: Part Two will explore the mythic journey of Paul Atreides as he unites with Chani and the Fremen while on a warpath of revenge against the conspirators who destroyed his family. Facing a choice between the love of his life and the fate of the known universe, he endeavors to prevent a terrible future only he can foresee.

programme.title: Madame Web
programme.desc: After showing signs of clairvoyance, New York City paramedic Cassandra Webb must confront revelations about her past and protect three young women from a mysterious adversary who wants them dead.

programme.title: John Farnham: Finding the Voice
programme.desc: In this first authorised biopic, we follow John Farnham's life from the quiet suburbs of Melbourne to '60s pop fame, through incredible highs and lows, and ultimately to record-breaking success as 'Australia's Voice'.

programme.title: David Holmes: The Boy Who Lived
programme.desc: A coming-of-age story of stuntman David Holmes, a prodigious teenage gymnast from Essex, England, who is selected to play Daniel Radcliffe's stunt double in the first Harry Potter film, when Daniel is just eleven. Over the next ten years, the two form an inextricable bond, but on the penultimate film a tragic accident on set leaves David paralyzed with a debilitating spinal injury, turning his world upside down. As Daniel and his closest stunt colleagues rally to support David and his family in their moment of need, it is David's extraordinary spirit of resilience that becomes their greatest source of strength and inspiration.

programme.title: Whale Nation
programme.desc: A humpback whale is beached on a remote shore. During the fight to save its life, we will discover the story of these extraordinary creatures, denizens of the world's oceans. Inspired by the best-selling book, and exquisitely narrated by Richard E. Grant, Whale Nation takes us on a journey of discovery into the unexplored territories and barely known society of whales, with their remarkable abilities and incredibly rich and complex social lives. A film about life in all its diversity, about human's place on Earth and the vital need for cooperation between the species that share our planet.

programme.title: Bookworm
programme.desc: 11-year-old Mildred's world is turned upside down when her estranged father, the washed-up magician Strawn Wise, comes to look after her and agrees to take her camping to find a mythological beast known as the Canterbury Panther.

programme.title: Barbie
programme.desc: To live in Barbie Land is to be a perfect being in a perfect place. Unless you have a full-on existential crisis. Or you're a Ken.

programme.title: The Heist Before Christmas
programme.desc: A Christmas-hating boy meets two Santas in the woods, one of whom claims to have fallen from his sleigh, but he is more interested in the other man when he learns he is on the run for robbing a bank.

programme.title: Unsung Hero
programme.desc: David Smallbone moves his family from Downunder to the States, searching for a brighter future after his successful music company collapses. With nothing more than their seven children, suitcases, and their love of music, David and his pregnant wife Helen set out to rebuild their lives. David and Helen begin to realise the musical prowess in their children, who would go on to become two of the most successful acts in Inspirational Music history.

programme.title: Allelujah
programme.desc: ALLELUJAH is a warm and deeply moving story about old age. When the geriatric ward in a small Yorkshire hospital is threatened with closure, the hospital decides to fight back by galvanizing the local community: they invite a news crew to film their preparations for a concert in honor of the hospital's most distinguished nurse. What could go wrong? ALLELUJAH celebrates the spirit of the elderly patients whilst paying tribute to the deep humanity of the medical staff battling with limited resources and ever-growing demand.

programme.title: The Miracle Club
programme.desc: Four women set out on a journey to Lourdes while confronting their pasts in search of a miracle.

programme.title: Dear Santa
programme.desc: A young boy who, in writing his yearly note to Santa, mixes up the letters and sends it to Satan instead.

programme.title: Ghostbusters: Frozen Empire
programme.desc: The Spenglers return to the New York City firehouse where the original Ghostbusters have taken ghost-busting to the next level. When the discovery of an ancient artefact unleashes an evil force, Ghostbusters new and old must unite to save the world.

programme.title: Ezra
programme.desc: Comedian Max co-parents autistic son Ezra with ex-wife Jenna. Faced with crucial decisions about Ezra's future, Max and Ezra go on a life-changing cross-country road trip.

programme.title: The Caine Mutiny Court-Martial
programme.desc: A naval officer stands trial for mutiny after taking command from a ship captain he feels is acting in an unstable way, putting in danger both the ship and its crew.

programme.title: The Great Escaper
programme.desc: Bernard Jordan, a World War II veteran, sneaks out of his care home to attend the 70th anniversary commemoration of the D-Day landings in Normandy.

programme.title: Dumb Money
programme.desc: The ultimate David versus Goliath story about the insane true story of everyday people who flipped the script on Wall Street and got rich by turning GameStop into the world's hottest company.

programme.title: The Holdovers
programme.desc: A curmudgeonly instructor at a prep school is forced to remain on campus during Christmas break to babysit a handful of students with nowhere to go.

programme.title: Bank of Dave
programme.desc: Based on the true-life experiences of Dave Fishwick, Bank of Dave tells the story of how a working-class Burnley man and self-made millionaire fought to set up a community bank.

programme.title: Sweet Dreams
programme.desc: Follows Morris, who must coach a misfit softball team of his fellow housemates during his mandatory stay at Sweet Dreams sober living in order to get his life back on track.

Facing a choice between the love of his life and the fate of the known universe, he endeavors to prevent a terrible future only he can foresee.

programme.title: The Fall Guy
programme.desc: He's a stuntman, and like everyone in the stunt community, he gets blown up, shot, crashed, thrown through windows and dropped from the highest of heights, all for our entertainment. And now, fresh off an almost career-ending accident, this working-class hero has to track down a missing movie star, solve a conspiracy and try to win back the love of his life while still doing his day job. What could possibly go right?

programme.title: Godzilla x Kong: The New Empire
programme.desc: Godzilla and the almighty Kong face a colossal threat hidden deep within the planet, challenging their very existence and the survival of the human race.

programme.title: Expend4bles
programme.desc: Armed with every weapon they can get their hands on and the skills to use them, The Expendables are the world's last line of defense and the team that gets called when all other options are off the table. But new team members with new styles and tactics are going to give "new blood" a new meaning.

programme.title: Meg 2: The Trench
programme.desc: An exploratory dive into the depths of the ocean of a research team spirals into chaos when a malevolent mining operation threatens their mission and forces them into a high-stakes battle for survival.

programme.title: Hypnotic
programme.desc: Determined to find his missing daughter, Austin detective, Danny Rourke instead finds himself spiraling down a rabbit hole while investigating a series of reality-bending bank robberies where he will ultimately call into question his most basic assumptions about everything and everyone in his world. Aided by Diana Cruz, an unnervingly gifted psychic, Rourke simultaneously pursues and is pursued by a lethal specter, the one man he believes holds the key to finding the missing girl only to discover more than he ever bargained for.

programme.title: The Passenger
programme.desc: Randy is perfectly content fading into the background. But when his co-worker Benson goes on a sudden and violent rampage leaving a trail of destruction in his wake, Randy is forced to face his fears and confront his troubled past in order to survive.

programme.title: Mercy Road
programme.desc: A flawed man commits a crime and learns how far he is willing to go to save his child.

programme.title: Something In The Water
programme.desc: A dream wedding turns into a nightmare when five girlfriends fight for their lives in open water.

programme.title: The Strangers: Chapter 1
programme.desc: When their car breaks down in an eerie small town, a couple must spend the night in a remote cabin. Panic ensues as they are terrorised by three masked strangers who strike with no mercy.

programme.title: Monkey Man
programme.desc: Inspired by the legend of Hanuman, an icon embodying strength and courage, Monkey Man stars Patel as Kid, an anonymous young man who ekes out a meager living in an underground fight club where, night after night, wearing a gorilla mask, he is beaten bloody by more popular fighters for cash.
